meta.title: Formula Atlas
meta.subtitle: A Practical Reference for Crop & Animal Production

chapters.title: Units, Conversions & Calculation Hygiene
chapters.intro:
Most calculation errors in agronomy and animal production are not due to the formulas themselves but to unit mismatches, rounding errors, or using industry vs. SI conventions inconsistently.
SI Units (default in this book): Length: meter (m) · Area: ha (10,000 m²) · Volume: liter (L) · Mass: kg · Temperature: °C · Energy: MJ
Agricultural practice units (still common): Acre (0.405 ha) · Inch of water · Cusec (ft³/s) · Bushel · lb, gal, cwt in livestock.
⚠  Pitfall: Many indices mix SI and non-SI in extension bulletins. Always normalize to SI, then convert back for reporting if needed.
Quick Reference
sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Converting to SI simplifies carbon/water footprinting, as most global protocols use SI.

chapters.title: Plant Population & Seed Rate
chapters.intro: Plant population and seed rate calculations are the foundation of crop establishment. Too low a population wastes land; too high creates competition, weak plants, and lodging.

formulas.name: Plant Population (PP)
formulas.formula: PP = (Area m² × 10,000) / (Row Spacing cm × Plant Spacing cm)
formulas.variables:
Area = m²
Spacing = cm
formulas.purpose: Estimate plants per hectare based on chosen spacing.
formulas.pitfall: Confusing cm and m; forgetting field mortality.
formulas.purpose_ar: تقدير عدد النباتات للهكتار بناءً على المسافة المحددة.
formulas.variables_ar:
المساحة = م²
المسافة = سم

formulas.name: Seed Rate (SR)
formulas.formula: SR = (Target Population × Test Weight g) / (1000 × 100 × Germination% × Purity%)
formulas.variables:
Test Weight = g per 1000 seeds
Germination & Purity = decimal (0.90 not 90)
formulas.purpose: Translate target population to kg seed to purchase.
formulas.example: 350,000 plants/ha, 35 g TW, 90% germ, 98% purity → SR = 126 kg/ha.
formulas.pitfall: Misplacing % as whole number instead of decimal.
formulas.purpose_ar: تحويل الكثافة المستهدفة إلى كجم من البذور للشراء.
formulas.variables_ar:
الوزن التجريبي = جرام لكل 1000 بذرة
الإنبات والنقاوة = رقم عشري (0.90 وليس 90)

formulas.name: Real Value of Seed (RVS)
formulas.variables:
Purity = % pure seed by weight
Germination = % viable
formulas.purpose: Proportion of seed lot that is viable and pure.
formulas.example: Purity 98%, germination 90% → RVS = 88.2%.
formulas.purpose_ar: النسبة المئوية لمجموعة البذور التي تكون حية ونقية.
formulas.variables_ar:
النقاوة = نسبة البذور النقية بالوزن
الإنبات = نسبة البذور الحية

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Seed replacement rate is a proxy for improved genetics adoption, but must be balanced with biodiversity conservation of local landraces.
decision_boxes: ▲  Decision: Order 5–10% more seed than calculated to cover field losses; calibrate seeder before planting.

chapters.title: Cropping Systems & Intercropping Indices
chapters.intro: Cropping system indices allow comparison of productivity, sustainability, and profitability of different field patterns, helping farmers and researchers choose optimal configurations.

formulas.name: Cropping Intensity (CI)
formulas.formula: CI (%) = Gross Cropped Area / Net Cultivable Area × 100
formulas.variables: Gross Cropped Area = total area sown across all seasons
formulas.purpose: Measures how many times land is cropped in a year.
formulas.example: 200 ha gross cropped on 100 ha land → CI = 200%.
formulas.purpose_ar: قياس عدد مرات زراعة الأرض في السنة.
formulas.variables_ar: المساحة المزروعة الإجمالية = المساحة الإجمالية المزروعة عبر جميع الموسمات

formulas.name: Land Equivalent Ratio (LER) ★ Key Index
formulas.variables:
Yia, Yib = intercrop yields of species a and b
Ysa, Ysb = sole crop yields
formulas.purpose: Measures yield advantage of intercropping vs. monoculture.
formulas.example: Maize/cowpea intercrop with LER = 1.35 → 35% land advantage.
formulas.pitfall: Always compare to same season sole crop controls.
formulas.purpose_ar: قياس ميزة المحصول في الزراعة المختلطة مقارنة بالزراعة الأحادية.
formulas.variables_ar:
Yia, Yib = محاصيل الزراعة المختلقة لنوعي أ و ب
Ysa, Ysb = محاصيل الزراعة الأحادية لنوعي أ و ب

formulas.name: Monetary Advantage Index (MAI)
formulas.variables:
LER = Land Equivalent Ratio
TMV = monetary value of total intercrop produce
formulas.purpose: Captures the financial advantage of intercropping over monoculture.
formulas.purpose_ar: يحلل الميزة المالية للزراعة المختلطة مقارنة بالزراعة الأحادية.
formulas.variables_ar:
LER = معدل المكافأة الأرضية
TMV = القيمة المادية الإجمالية لمنتج الزراعة المختلقة

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Diversity indices (DI, HDI) link directly to resilience against pests, climate variability, and market shocks.

chapters.title: Fertilizer & Nutrient Management
chapters.intro: Nutrient management is central to productivity and sustainability. Correct fertilizer calculation avoids both underfeeding (yield loss) and overuse (wasted input, pollution).

formulas.name: Fertilizer Requirement
formulas.formula: Fertilizer (kg/ha) = 100 × Recommended Rate (kg/ha) / Nutrient% in product
formulas.variables:
Recommended rate = nutrient element (N, P, K)
Nutrient % = label value
formulas.purpose: Convert nutrient requirement to commercial fertilizer quantity.
formulas.example: 120 kg N/ha needed; urea 46% N → 261 kg urea/ha.
formulas.pitfall: Confusing element rate with oxide form (P vs P₂O₅).
formulas.purpose_ar: تحويل احتياجات العناصر الغذائية إلى كمية الأسمدة التجارية.
formulas.variables_ar:
المعدل الموصى به = عنصر غذائي (N, P, K)
نسبة العنصر الغذائي = القيمة الموجودة على العبوة

formulas.name: Agronomic Efficiency (AE)
formulas.formula: AE = (Yf − Yu) / N_applied  [kg yield / kg nutrient]
formulas.variables:
Yf = yield with fertilizer
Yu = yield without fertilizer
N_applied = kg/ha
formulas.purpose: Yield gain per kg of nutrient applied.
formulas.example: Yf=5 t/ha, Yu=3 t/ha, N=100 kg/ha → AE = 20 kg grain/kg N.
formulas.pitfall: Always specify which NUE metric is reported; they differ substantially.
formulas.purpose_ar: زيادة المحصول لكل كجم من العنصر الغذائي المطبق.
formulas.variables_ar:
Yf = المحصول مع استخدام الأسمدة
Yu = المحصول بدون استخدام الأسمدة
N_applied = كجم/هكتار

formulas.name: Apparent Nutrient Recovery (ANR)
formulas.variables: NUf, NUu = nutrient uptake with/without fertilizer (kg/ha)
formulas.purpose: Percentage of applied nutrient recovered by the crop.
formulas.purpose_ar: النسبة المئوية للعنصر الغذائي المطبق الذي يسترده المحصول.
formulas.variables_ar: NUf, NUu = امتصاص العنصر الغذائي مع/بدون استخدام الأسمدة (كجم/هكتار)

formulas.name: Partial Factor Productivity (PFP)
formulas.formula: PFP = Y / N_applied  [kg yield / kg nutrient]
formulas.variables: Y = total yield including soil contribution
formulas.purpose: Total yield per unit of nutrient applied; broadest NUE measure.
formulas.example: Y=5 t/ha, N=100 kg → PFP = 50 kg/kg.
formulas.purpose_ar: المحصول الإجمالي لكل وحدة من العنصر الغذائي المطبق; أوسع مقاييس كفاءة استخدام العنصر الغذائي.
formulas.variables_ar: Y = المحصول الإجمالي بما في ذلك مساهمة التربة

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: High NUE reduces runoff, leaching, and GHG emissions while saving input costs.

chapters.title: Weed, Pest & Disease Management
chapters.intro: Quantitative indices allow researchers and practitioners to compare treatments, assess control effectiveness, and guide integrated pest management (IPM) decisions.

formulas.name: Weed Control Efficiency (WCE)
formulas.variables:
WDc = weed dry weight in control
WDt = weed dry weight in treatment
formulas.purpose: Measures how effectively a treatment reduces weed biomass.
formulas.example: Control=500 g/m², treatment=150 g/m² → WCE = 70%.
formulas.purpose_ar: يقيس مدى فعالية العلاج في تقليل كتلة الأعشاب الجافة.
formulas.variables_ar:
WDc = وزن الأعشاب الجافة في المجموعة الضابطة
WDt = وزن الأعشاب الجافة في المجموعة المعالجة

formulas.name: Economic Threshold Level (ETL)
formulas.variables:
C = cost of control
V = crop market value/unit
I = injury per pest
D = pest density
K = proportion of damage avoided
formulas.purpose: Determines when pest control becomes economically justifiable.
formulas.example: Spray only when pest density exceeds the ETL threshold.
formulas.pitfall: ETL is dynamic — recalculate when crop prices or pest densities change.
formulas.purpose_ar: يحدد متى يصبح مكافحة الآفات مبرراً اقتصادياً.
formulas.variables_ar:
C = تكلفة السيطرة
V = قيمة السوق للمحصول/الوحدة
I = إصابة لكل آفة
D = كثافة الآفة
K = النسبة المئوية للضرر المُتَجَنَّب

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: PUE and ETL emphasize minimizing pesticide use to protect beneficial organisms, reduce residues, and delay resistance.

chapters.title: Irrigation & Water Management
chapters.intro: Water is often the most limiting factor in agriculture. Efficient irrigation ensures crops get what they need without waste, salinity buildup, or environmental damage.

formulas.name: Net Irrigation Requirement (NIR)
formulas.variables:
ETc = crop evapotranspiration (mm)
Pe = effective rainfall (mm)
formulas.purpose: Net water needed by the crop from irrigation.
formulas.example: ETc=450 mm, Pe=120 mm → NIR = 330 mm.
formulas.purpose_ar: المياه الصافية التي يحتاجها المحصول من الري.
formulas.variables_ar:
ETc = التبخر النتحي للمحصول (ملم)
Pe = الأمطار الفعالة (ملم)

formulas.name: Gross Irrigation Requirement (GIR)
formulas.variables: Ea = application efficiency (decimal, e.g. 0.75)
formulas.purpose: Total water to apply at field level, accounting for losses.
formulas.example: NIR=330 mm, Ea=0.75 → GIR = 440 mm.
formulas.pitfall: Forgetting that Ea < 1 means GIR always exceeds NIR.
formulas.purpose_ar: إجمالي المياه التي تطبق على مستوى الحقل، مع الأخذ في الاعتبار الفقد.
formulas.variables_ar: Ea = كفاءة التطبيق (عشرية، مثلاً 0.75)

formulas.name: Water Use Efficiency (WUE)
formulas.formula: WUE = Yield / Water used  [kg/mm or kg/m³]
formulas.variables:
Yield = economic yield (kg/ha)
Water used = total ET or applied irrigation
formulas.purpose: Measures crop productivity per unit of water consumed.
formulas.example: 5 t grain/ha with 450 mm water → WUE = 11.1 kg/mm.
formulas.purpose_ar: يقيس إنتاجية المحصول لكل وحدة من المياه المستهلكة.
formulas.variables_ar:
المحصول = المحصول الاقتصادي (كجم/هكتار)
المياه المستخدمة = إجمالي التبخر النتحي أو الري المطبق

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Improving Ea and CU reduces groundwater depletion and energy use in pumping while improving yields.

chapters.title: Soil Properties & Soil Health
chapters.intro: Soil is the foundation of both crop and livestock systems. These indices evaluate physical, chemical, and biological properties for sustainable productivity.

formulas.name: Bulk Density (BD)
formulas.formula: BD = Oven-dry soil mass / Total soil volume  [g/cm³]
formulas.variables: Typical range: 1.1–1.6 g/cm³ for mineral soils
formulas.purpose: Indicator of compaction and porosity.
formulas.example: Mass 130 g, volume 100 cm³ → BD = 1.3 g/cm³.
formulas.pitfall: Use undisturbed core samples only.
formulas.purpose_ar: مؤشر للانضغاطية والمسامية.
formulas.variables_ar: المدى النموذجي: 1.1-1.6 غ/سم³ للتربة المعدنية

formulas.name: Porosity
formulas.variables:
BD = bulk density
PD = particle density (≈2.65 g/cm³ for mineral soil)
formulas.purpose: Indicates soil aeration and water holding capacity.
formulas.purpose_ar: تشير إلى تهوية التربة وقدرتها على الاحتفاظ بالمياه.
formulas.variables_ar:
BD = الكثافة الظاهرية
PD = الكثافة الجسيمية (≈2.65 غ/سم³ للتربة المعدنية)

formulas.name: Sodium Adsorption Ratio (SAR)
formulas.variables:
All ions in mmol/L or meq/L
SAR > 13 = sodicity hazard
formulas.purpose: Assess irrigation water quality and sodicity risk.
formulas.purpose_ar: تقييم جودة مياه الري ومخاطر الصوديوم.
formulas.variables_ar:
جميع الأيونات في ملليمول/لتر أو مكافئ ملليمول/لتر
SAR > 13 = خطر الصوديوم

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Improving SOC improves fertility, resilience to drought, and carbon sequestration, contributing to climate mitigation.

chapters.title: Crop Growth & Yield Indices
chapters.intro: Growth and yield indices allow researchers to quantify how efficiently plants convert resources into biomass and grain. Critical for evaluating varieties, management practices, and stress effects.

formulas.name: Absolute Growth Rate (AGR)
formulas.variables:
W = plant dry weight (g)
t = time (days)
formulas.purpose: Increase in weight per unit time; simple, intuitive growth measure.
formulas.example: W₁=10 g at day 20, W₂=40 g at day 40 → AGR = 1.5 g/day.
formulas.purpose_ar: زيادة الوزن لكل وحدة زمنية؛ مقياس بسيط وواضح للنمو.
formulas.variables_ar:
W = وزن النبات الجاف (غرام)
t = الوقت (أيام)

formulas.name: Harvest Index (HI)
formulas.variables:
Economic yield = grain, fiber, tuber (marketed)
Biological yield = total above-ground biomass
formulas.purpose: Efficiency of converting biomass to marketable product.
formulas.example: Grain=5 t/ha, biomass=12 t/ha → HI = 41.7%.
formulas.pitfall: Typical range 30–50% for cereals; below 30% indicates poor partitioning.
formulas.purpose_ar: كفاءة تحويل الكتلة الحيوية إلى منتج قابل للتسويق.
formulas.variables_ar:
المحصول الاقتصادي = الحبوب، الألياف، الدرن (مُسَوَّق)
المحصول البيولوجي = إجمالي الكتلة الحيوية فوق سطح الأرض

formulas.name: Growing Degree Days (GDD)
formulas.variables:
Tbase = biological zero (e.g. 10°C for maize)
Sum daily across growth period
formulas.purpose: Heat unit accumulation governing phenology and maturity timing.
formulas.example: Tmax=28°C, Tmin=16°C, Tbase=10°C → GDD/day = 12.
formulas.pitfall: Use crop-specific Tbase; avoid truncating negative values inconsistently.

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: LAI, HI, and HUE are critical for breeding climate-smart crops that use light, water, and heat more efficiently.

chapters.title: Remote Sensing & Digital Agronomy
chapters.intro: Remote sensing offers rapid, non-destructive monitoring of crops at field to regional scales. Indices from satellite or drone imagery help assess vegetation health, biomass, stress, and yield potential.

formulas.name: NDVI – Normalized Difference Vegetation Index
formulas.variables:
NIR = near-infrared reflectance
Red = red band reflectance
Range: −1 to +1
formulas.purpose: Most widely used vegetation vigor index; correlates with LAI and biomass.
formulas.example: NIR=0.65, Red=0.25 → NDVI = 0.44 (moderate-good cover).
formulas.pitfall: Saturates at high LAI; use EVI for dense canopies.

formulas.name: SAVI – Soil-Adjusted Vegetation Index
formulas.variables:
L = soil adjustment factor (0.5 default)
Reduces soil background effect
formulas.purpose: Corrects NDVI for bare soil influence in sparse canopies.
formulas.example: Same bands as NDVI, L=0.5; preferred for < 15% cover.

formulas.name: VHI – Vegetation Health Index
formulas.variables:
VCI = Vegetation Condition Index (greenness vs history)
TCI = Temperature Condition Index
a = weight (typically 0.5)
formulas.purpose: Combines greenness and thermal stress into a drought composite.
formulas.example: VCI=37.5%, TCI=50%, a=0.5 → VHI = 43.75 (moderate stress).

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Remote sensing reduces reliance on destructive sampling and enables early warning of stress, supporting climate resilience.

chapters.title: Animal Nutrition & Feed Efficiency
chapters.intro: Nutrition is the backbone of animal productivity. Proper feed formulation ensures growth, reproduction, milk, meat, and egg yield while minimizing waste and costs.

formulas.name: Dry Matter Intake (DMI)
formulas.variables: Typical: 2.5–4% of body weight for dairy cows
formulas.purpose: Quantifies actual dry matter consumed; basis for ration formulation.
formulas.example: Cow offered 25 kg DM, refused 3 kg → DMI = 22 kg/day.

formulas.name: Feed Conversion Ratio (FCR)
formulas.formula: FCR = Feed intake / Weight gain  [kg feed/kg gain]
formulas.variables:
Lower FCR = better efficiency
Typical: broilers 1.6–2.0, beef 6–8
formulas.purpose: Industry benchmark for feed efficiency.
formulas.example: Lamb: 40 kg feed, 10 kg gain → FCR = 4.0.
formulas.pitfall: Must specify the feed basis (as-fed vs dry matter).

formulas.name: Energy-Corrected Milk (ECM)
formulas.variables: Milk = kg/day; Fat, Protein = kg/day (not %)
formulas.purpose: Standardizes milk yield for fat and protein content to allow fair comparisons.
formulas.example: 25 kg milk, 0.95 kg fat, 0.80 kg protein → ECM = 32.7 kg/day.
formulas.pitfall: Convert % to kg by multiplying by milk yield first.

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Improving FCR reduces feed demand, land pressure, and methane emissions per unit of product.

chapters.title: Growth Performance & Body Condition
chapters.intro: Measuring animal growth and condition is critical for monitoring herd performance, adjusting nutrition, and making breeding and culling decisions.

formulas.name: Average Daily Gain (ADG)
formulas.formula: ADG = (Final weight − Initial weight) / Days  [kg/day or g/day]
formulas.variables: Weigh animals at consistent times (morning, after overnight fast)
formulas.purpose: Standard growth measurement across species and production systems.
formulas.example: Calf: 320 kg final − 200 kg initial, 120 days → ADG = 1.0 kg/day.
formulas.pitfall: Use multiple weighing periods to smooth measurement error.

formulas.name: Kleiber Ratio (KR)
formulas.formula: KR = ADG / Metabolic weight  where Metabolic weight = BW^0.75
formulas.variables:
BW = body weight (kg)
Allows growth comparison across breeds/sizes
formulas.purpose: Growth efficiency index independent of animal size.

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Selecting animals with high ADG and Kleiber Ratio improves productivity while reducing resource use and emissions per unit product.

chapters.title: Reproduction & Breeding Metrics
chapters.intro: Reproductive efficiency drives productivity in livestock systems. Breeding indices guide genetic improvement, while reproductive rates determine profitability.

formulas.name: Conception Rate (CR)
formulas.variables: Target: >60% for dairy cattle with good heat detection
formulas.purpose: Efficiency of a single insemination or mating event.
formulas.example: 100 cows inseminated, 45 conceived → CR = 45% (below target).
formulas.pitfall: Low CR may reflect heat detection failure, not poor fertility.

formulas.name: Calving Interval (CI)
formulas.variables:
Days open = days from calving to next conception
Target: 365–400 days
formulas.purpose: Fundamental dairy efficiency metric linking reproduction and lactation.
formulas.example: Gestation 280 days, days open 90 → CI = 370 days (good).
formulas.purpose_ar: مؤشر أساسي لفعالية الحليب يربط بين التكاثر والرضاعة.
formulas.variables_ar:
Days open = الأيام من الولادة إلى الحمل التالي
الهدف: 365-400 يوم

formulas.name: Genetic Gain (ΔG)
formulas.variables:
i = selection intensity
r = accuracy of selection
σA = additive genetic SD
L = generation interval (years)
formulas.purpose: Predicts annual genetic progress from a selection program.
formulas.example: i=1.2, r=0.7, σA=10, L=5 yrs → ΔG = 1.68 units/year.
formulas.purpose_ar: يتنبأ بالتقدم الوراثي السنني من برنامج الاختيار.
formulas.variables_ar:
i = شدة الاختيار
r = دقة الاختيار
σA = الانحراف المعياري الوراثي الإضافي
L = فترة الجيل (سنوات)

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Improving reproductive efficiency reduces replacement pressure, methane emissions, and resource use per unit of milk/meat.

chapters.title: Dairy Production Metrics
chapters.intro: Standardized milk yield indices allow fair comparison across cows, breeds, and management systems, and help optimize feeding and breeding strategies.

formulas.name: Fat-Corrected Milk (FCM)
formulas.variables:
Milk = kg/day
Fat = kg/day
formulas.purpose: Standardizes milk yield to 4% fat basis for comparisons.
formulas.purpose_ar: ي标准化 إنتاج الحليح إلى أساس 4% دهون للتباين.
formulas.variables_ar:
Milk = كجم/يوم
Fat = كجم/يوم

formulas.name: Persistency Index (PI)
formulas.formula: PI (%) = Milk yield later / Peak yield × 100
formulas.variables: Compare a standard reference period to peak production
formulas.purpose: Measures how well cows sustain production after peak lactation.
formulas.example: Peak 45 kg/day, month 7 yield 32 kg/day → PI = 71%.
formulas.purpose_ar: يقيس مدى قدرة الأبقار على الحفاظ على الإنتاج بعد ذروة الرضاعة.
formulas.variables_ar: يتم مقارنة فترة مرجعية قياسية مع الإنتاج ذروة

formulas.name: Somatic Cell Score (SCS)
formulas.variables:
SCC = somatic cell count (cells/mL)
SCS < 4.5 = healthy udder
formulas.purpose: Log-transformed SCC; more normally distributed for genetic analysis.
formulas.example: SCC=200,000 → SCS = log₂(2)+3 = 4.0 (acceptable).
formulas.purpose_ar: عدد الخلايا الجسدية المحول إلى لوغاريتم; توزيعه أكثر طبيعية للتحليل الوراثي.
formulas.variables_ar:
SCC = عدد الخلايا الجسدية (خلية/مل)
SCS < 4.5 = ضرع صحي

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Improving DER and PI reduces feed use, methane, and land pressure per unit of milk.

chapters.title: Meat Production & Carcass Indices
chapters.intro: Carcass indices provide objective measures of yield, quality, and efficiency, supporting breeding, nutrition, and market decisions.

formulas.name: Dressing Percentage (DP)
formulas.variables:
Cattle: 55–65%
Sheep/goats: 45–55%
Broilers: 70–75%
formulas.purpose: Basic measure of proportion of live weight becoming saleable carcass.
formulas.example: Sheep: live 40 kg, carcass 20 kg → DP = 50%.
formulas.pitfall: Confounding effects: gut fill, hide, breed type.
formulas.purpose_ar: مؤشر أساسي لنسبة الوزن الحي الذي يصبح جثة للبيع.
formulas.variables_ar:
الماشية: 55-65%
الغنم/الماعز: 45-55%
الدجاج المبيد: 70-75%

formulas.name: Broiler Performance Efficiency Factor (BPEF / EBI)
formulas.variables:
Livability = % surviving
BW = body weight at slaughter
FCR = feed conversion ratio
formulas.purpose: Industry composite index of broiler production efficiency.
formulas.example: 95% livability, 2.0 kg BW, 40 days, FCR 1.8 → EBI = 263.
formulas.name_ar: عامل كفاءة أداء الدجاج المبيد (BPEF / EBI)
formulas.purpose_ar: مؤشر تجمع صناعي لكفاءة إنتاج الدجاج المبيد.
formulas.variables_ar:
Livability = النسبة المئوية للبقاء
BW = الوزن الجسمي عند الذبح
FCR = نسبة التحويل الغذائي

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Improving FCR, DP, and EBI reduces feed use, water, and land footprint per unit of meat.

chapters.title: Poultry Production Metrics
chapters.intro: Poultry systems are among the most efficient converters of feed into animal protein. Monitoring egg and meat indices is essential for benchmarking performance and sustainability.

formulas.name: Hen-Day Egg Production (HDEP)
formulas.formula: HDEP (%) = Eggs produced / Hens present that day × 100
formulas.variables:
Measured daily
Reflects current flock productivity
formulas.purpose: Daily productivity benchmark excluding mortality effects.
formulas.example: 950 eggs from 1,000 hens present → HDEP = 95%.
formulas.pitfall: Does not account for cumulative mortality; use HHEP for that.
formulas.purpose_ar: مؤشر إنتاجية يومي يتجاهل تأثير الوفيات.
formulas.variables_ar:
يتم قياسه يوميا
يعكس إنتاجية قطيع الحالي

formulas.name: Haugh Unit (HU)
formulas.variables:
H = albumen height (mm)
W = egg weight (g)
HU > 72 = Grade AA; < 60 = Grade B
formulas.purpose: Standard measure of egg interior quality (freshness).
formulas.example: H=8 mm, W=60 g → HU ≈ 88 (excellent quality).
formulas.purpose_ar: مؤشر قياسي لجودة داخل البيض (الطازجة).
formulas.variables_ar:
H = ارتفاع البياض (ملم)
W = وزن البيضة (جم)
HU > 72 = الدرجة AA; < 60 = الدرجة B

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Improved FCR in poultry has the lowest environmental footprint per unit protein among all livestock — ongoing optimization matters.

chapters.title: Climate-Smart Agriculture Metrics
chapters.intro: Climate variability is the dominant risk in modern farming. These indices quantify heat/cold stress, aridity, and water–carbon–energy interactions.

formulas.name: Aridity Index (AI)
formulas.variables:
AI < 0.05 = hyper-arid
0.05–0.2 = arid
0.2–0.5 = semi-arid
0.5–0.65 = dry sub-humid
formulas.purpose: Classifies climate zones and guides crop selection and water management.
formulas.example: P=300 mm, PET=800 mm → AI = 0.375 (semi-arid).
formulas.purpose_ar: يصنف مناخات المناطق ويوجه اختيار المحاصيل وإدارة المياه.
formulas.variables_ar:
AI < 0.05 = جاف جدا
0.05-0.2 = جاف
0.2-0.5 = شبه جاف
0.5-0.65 = شبه رطب جاف

formulas.name: Moisture Deficit Index (MDI)
formulas.variables:
PET = potential evapotranspiration
P = precipitation
Range 0–1
formulas.purpose: Proportion of water demand not met by rainfall.
formulas.example: PET=700, P=300 → MDI = 0.57 (moderate deficit).

chapters.title: Farm Economics
chapters.intro: Economic indices translate agronomic and production data into profitability and investment metrics for decision-making at farm and policy level.

formulas.name: Benefit-Cost Ratio (B:C)
formulas.formula: B:C = Total gross returns / Total costs
formulas.variables:
B:C > 1 = profitable
Include all variable and fixed costs
formulas.purpose: Simple profitability assessment for farm enterprises.
formulas.example: Returns $5,000, Costs $3,500 → B:C = 1.43 (profitable).
formulas.pitfall: Ignores time value of money; use NPV for multi-year investments.

formulas.name: Gross Margin (GM)
formulas.formula: GM = Gross revenue − Variable costs  [$/ha or $/head]
formulas.variables: Does not deduct fixed/overhead costs
formulas.purpose: Most common farm enterprise comparison metric.
formulas.example: Revenue $4,000/ha, variable costs $1,800/ha → GM = $2,200/ha.

formulas.name: Net Present Value (NPV)
formulas.variables:
CFt = cash flow in year t
r = discount rate
NPV > 0 = invest
formulas.purpose: Time-adjusted profitability for capital investments (irrigation, livestock housing).
formulas.example: Use 10–12% discount rate for agricultural investments in developing markets.

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Gross margin analysis combined with environmental cost accounting is the pathway toward true sustainability accounting in farming systems.

chapters.title: Experimental Design & Agricultural Statistics
chapters.intro:
Sound experimental design is the backbone of credible research. This chapter provides quickrefs for standard designs and statistical analysis in crop and animal science.
Quick Reference

rows: Blocks control one direction of variation
Both factors in strips across field

chapters.title: Precision Agriculture & Digital Tools
chapters.intro:
Precision agriculture integrates GPS, remote sensing, IoT sensors, and data analytics to optimize inputs and management at sub-field resolution.
Quick Reference

chapters.title: Agroecology & Ecosystem Services
chapters.intro: Agroecological approaches quantify biodiversity, soil biology, and ecosystem services as part of holistic farm system assessment.

formulas.name: Shannon Diversity Index (H')
formulas.variables:
pi = proportion of species i in community
Higher H' = greater diversity
formulas.purpose: Measures species diversity combining richness and evenness.

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Integrating biodiversity indices into farm management connects productivity with ecosystem resilience, beneficial insect populations, and soil biota health.

chapters.title: Case Studies
chapters.intro:
Case studies illustrate how integrated analysis of indices leads to better management, profitability, and sustainability across real farming systems.
Quick Reference

rows: Reduced fertilizer costs, better feed availability
DER=1.4, CIM=7,800 L/yr, CF=1.8 kg CO₂/L
Higher food security, better soil fertility

chapters.title: Glossary
chapters.intro: Quick Reference

rows: Rate of biomass increase per unit time
Ratio of leaf area to ground area
Mass of dry soil per unit volume
Feed consumed per unit of weight gain
Visual scoring of animal fat reserves
Milk yield adjusted for fat and protein
Feed consumed on dry matter basis

chapters.title: Reporting Checklist
chapters.intro:
Use this checklist for every major calculation to ensure reproducibility and decision quality:
Quick Reference

rows: Frame the problem — what decision depends on this number?
Write equation with units for every term
List data sources (date, method, instrument, lab)
State all assumptions (moisture basis, temperature, stage)
Interpret result against threshold or benchmark
Record recommended action, responsible person, and timeline

chapters.title: Soil Microbiology & Biological Health Metrics
chapters.intro: Soil biological properties are increasingly recognized as critical indicators of long-term productivity. These indices quantify the living component of soils — the microbial engine that drives nutrient cycling, organic matter decomposition, and disease suppression.

formulas.name: Microbial Biomass Carbon (MBC)
formulas.formula: MBC = (C_fumigated − C_control) / kEC  [mg C kg⁻¹ soil]
formulas.variables:
kEC = extraction efficiency factor (0.45 commonly used)
Measured by chloroform fumigation-extraction (CFE)
Typical range: 100–1500 mg C/kg
formulas.purpose: Estimates the mass of living microbial cells in soil; sensitive early indicator of soil quality change.
formulas.example: Fumigated soil C = 250 mg/kg, control = 140 mg/kg → MBC = (250−140)/0.45 = 244 mg C/kg.
formulas.pitfall: Highly sensitive to sampling date, soil moisture, and temperature — standardize conditions.

formulas.name: Metabolic Quotient (qCO₂)
formulas.formula: qCO₂ = Soil respiration / MBC  [mg CO₂-C g⁻¹ MBC h⁻¹]
formulas.variables:
Soil respiration = CO₂ evolved per unit time
MBC = microbial biomass carbon
Lower qCO₂ = more efficient microbial community
formulas.purpose: Measures metabolic efficiency of soil microbial community. High qCO₂ indicates stress.
formulas.example: Respiration = 0.08 mg CO₂-C/g/h, MBC = 400 mg/kg → qCO₂ = 0.0002.

formulas.name: Soil Enzyme Activity Index (EAI)
formulas.formula: EAI = (Enzyme activity observed / Enzyme activity reference) × 100
formulas.variables:
Key enzymes: urease, phosphatase, dehydrogenase, β-glucosidase
Reference = optimal baseline for soil type
formulas.purpose: Composite of enzyme activities reflecting nutrient cycling capacity.
formulas.example: Dehydrogenase = 45 μg TPF/g/24h; reference = 60 → EAI = 75% (reduced activity).

formulas.name: Fungal:Bacterial (F:B) Ratio
formulas.formula: F:B = Fungal biomass / Bacterial biomass  [from PLFA or qPCR]
formulas.variables:
Estimated from phospholipid fatty acid (PLFA) profiles
F:B > 1 common in undisturbed or high-OM soils
Fungi dominate carbon cycling in agroforestry
formulas.purpose: Indicates decomposition pathways and soil disturbance history.
formulas.example: Fungal PLFA = 22 nmol/g, bacterial PLFA = 44 nmol/g → F:B = 0.50.

formulas.name: Microbial Biomass Nitrogen (MBN)
formulas.formula: MBN = (N_fumigated − N_control) / kEN  [mg N kg⁻¹ soil]
formulas.variables:
kEN = 0.54 (extraction coefficient)
MBN:MBC ratio reflects microbial C:N constraint
formulas.purpose: Estimates nitrogen immobilized in microbial biomass; important for N release timing.
formulas.example: MBC:MBN ratio < 5 → excess N release; > 15 → N immobilization risk.

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: MBC and qCO₂ respond rapidly to management changes — they are leading indicators of soil health trajectories years before SOC changes are detectable.

chapters.title: Phenology & Crop Staging
chapters.intro:
Standardized growth stage systems allow precise communication about crop development timing, linking management decisions (fertilization, irrigation, pest control) to specific developmental windows where they have maximum impact.
The BBCH (Biologische Bundesanstalt, Bundessortenamt, CHemische Industrie) decimal scale uses two digits: the first denotes the principal growth stage (0–9), the second the secondary stage within it.
Quick Reference

formulas.name: Leaf Appearance Rate (LAR)
formulas.formula: LAR = Number of leaves / Thermal time elapsed  [leaves °Cd⁻¹]
formulas.variables:
Thermal time = cumulative GDD from emergence
Species-specific: maize ≈ 1 leaf per 60–80°Cd
formulas.purpose: Predicts leaf number at any thermal time; used to time management interventions.
formulas.example: Maize at 400 GDD: LAR = 0.014 leaves/°Cd → ≈ 5.6 leaves expected.
formulas.purpose_ar: يُتنبأ بعدد الأوراق في أي وقت حراري؛ يُستخدم لتحديد وقت تدخلات الإدارة.
formulas.variables_ar:
الوقت الحراري = G 누적 من وقت الظهور
خاصية النوع: الذرة ≈ 1 ورقة لكل 60-80 درجة مئوية يوم

formulas.name: Phyllochron
formulas.formula: Phyllochron = Thermal time between successive leaf appearances  [°Cd leaf⁻¹]
formulas.variables:
Inverse of LAR
Maize: 45–80°Cd
Wheat: 90–110°Cd
Used in crop simulation models
formulas.purpose: Fundamental parameter for crop development models (CERES, APSIM).
formulas.example: Two leaves appear over 120°Cd → phyllochron = 60°Cd/leaf.
formulas.purpose_ar: معلمة أساسية لنماذج تطوير المحاصيل (CERES, APSIM).
formulas.variables_ar:
عكس معدل LAR
الذرة: 45-80 درجة مئوية يوم
القمح: 90-110 درجة مئوية يوم
يُستخدم في نماذج محاكاة المحاصيل

rows: Early N top-dressing, tiller count assessment
Second N split, growth regulator application

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Phenology-based management reduces input waste — fungicide applications at BBCH 39–59 in wheat are 30–50% more effective than calendar-based timing.

chapters.title: Greenhouse & Protected Agriculture Metrics
chapters.intro: Controlled environment agriculture (CEA) requires a different set of metrics from field production. Optimization of light, CO₂, temperature, humidity, and nutrient solution are the primary levers.

formulas.name: Daily Light Integral (DLI)
formulas.variables:
PPFD = photosynthetic photon flux density (μmol m⁻² s⁻¹)
Photoperiod = hours of light per day
Target: leafy greens 12–17; tomato 20–30 mol/m²/d
formulas.purpose: Quantifies total photosynthetically active radiation received in a day; main driver of greenhouse crop yield.
formulas.example: PPFD = 250 μmol/m²/s for 16 hours → DLI = 250×3600×16/1,000,000 = 14.4 mol/m²/d.
formulas.pitfall: Do not confuse PPFD (instantaneous) with DLI (daily sum).
formulas.purpose_ar: يُقيس الإشعاع الفوتوسينثتيك النشط المستلم في يوم واحد؛ المحرك الرئيسي لغلة المحاصيل في البيوت المحمية.
formulas.variables_ar:
PPFD = كثافة تدفق الفوتونات الفوتوسينثتيك (μmol m⁻² s⁻¹)
فترة الضوء = ساعات الضوء في اليوم
الهدف: للخضروات الورقية 12-17؛ الطماطم 20-30 مول/م²/يوم

formulas.name: Vapor Pressure Deficit (VPD)
formulas.variables:
T = air temperature (°C)
RH = relative humidity (%)
Target: 0.8–1.5 kPa for most crops
formulas.purpose: Drives transpiration and stomatal behavior; key climate parameter for greenhouse management.
formulas.example: T=22°C, RH=70%: SVP=2.64 kPa → VPD = 2.64×0.30 = 0.79 kPa (low-moderate).
formulas.pitfall: High VPD (>2 kPa) causes stomatal closure and yield loss; low VPD (<0.4 kPa) promotes disease.
formulas.purpose_ar: يُحرك النتح وسلوك الثغور؛ معلمة مناخية أساسية لإدارة البيوت المحمية.
formulas.variables_ar:
T = درجة حرارة الهواء (°C)
RH = الرطوبة النسبية (%)
الهدف: 0.8-1.5 كيلوباسكال لأغلب المحاصيل

formulas.name: CO₂ Enrichment Efficiency (CEE)
formulas.formula: CEE = (Yield_enriched − Yield_ambient) / CO₂ added  [kg yield / kg CO₂]
formulas.variables:
Ambient CO₂ ≈ 420 ppm; enrichment target 800–1200 ppm for C3 crops
C4 crops (maize, sorghum) do not respond significantly
formulas.purpose: Measures economic benefit of CO₂ enrichment against gas cost.
formulas.example: Tomato: yield gain 3 kg/m², CO₂ used 0.6 kg/m² → CEE = 5 kg tomato/kg CO₂.
formulas.purpose_ar: يُقيس الفائدة الاقتصادية لإثراء CO₂ مقابل تكلفة الغاز.
formulas.variables_ar:
CO₂ المحيطي ≈ 420 جزء في المليون؛ هدف الإثراء 800-1200 جزء في المليون لمحاصيل C3
محاصيل C4 (الذرة، الذرة الرفيعة) لا تستجيب بشكل كبير

formulas.name: Nutrient Solution EC & pH Management
formulas.formula: EC target (dS/m) varies by crop growth stage and species
formulas.variables:
Seedling: 0.8–1.2 dS/m
Vegetative: 1.5–2.5 dS/m
Fruiting: 2.0–4.0 dS/m
pH target: 5.8–6.2 for most crops
formulas.purpose: EC guides nutrient concentration; deviation indicates dilution (overwatering) or buildup (underwatering).
formulas.example: EC drops from 2.2 to 1.6 dS/m → plants consuming water faster than nutrients; increase feed concentration.
formulas.name_ar: إدارة EC و pH محلول المغذيات
formulas.purpose_ar: EC توجه تركيز المغذيات؛ الانحراف يشير إلى التخفيف (سقي مفرط) أو التراكم (سقي ناقص).
formulas.variables_ar:
الشتلة: 0.8-1.2 dS/m
النمو الخضري: 1.5-2.5 dS/m
الإثمار: 2.0-4.0 dS/m
الهدف pH: 5.8-6.2 لأغلب المحاصيل

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: CEA uses 70–95% less water than field production and eliminates pesticide runoff — metrics that quantify these savings are key for sustainability reporting.

chapters.title: Agroforestry Systems Metrics
chapters.intro: Agroforestry integrates trees with crops and/or livestock on the same land unit. The interactions — competition and complementarity for light, water, and nutrients — require adapted metrics beyond standard intercropping indices.

formulas.name: Shade Equivalency Ratio (SER)
formulas.variables:
SER > 1 = shade-tolerant crop benefits from tree shade
SER < 1 = light competition dominant
formulas.purpose: Measures the yield response of an understory crop to tree-induced shade.
formulas.example: Coffee under Acacia: 1.8 t/ha vs 1.4 t/ha in full sun → SER = 1.29.
formulas.purpose_ar: يُقيس استجابة غلة المحصول السفلي الظليل الناتج عن الأشجار.
formulas.variables_ar:
SER > 1 = المحصول الظلوم يستفيد من ظل الأشجار
SER < 1 = المنافسة الضووية هي المهيمنة

formulas.name: Agroforestry LER (AF-LER)
formulas.variables:
Ytree = timber, fruit, or fodder yield from trees
Stree = tree monoculture yield
Sum all components present in system
formulas.purpose: Extends the standard LER to include tree products; AF-LER > 1 confirms land use advantage.
formulas.example: Timber partial LER=0.6, crop partial LER=0.9 → AF-LER = 1.5 (50% land advantage).
formulas.name_ar: كفاءة استخدام الأرض في الزراعة الحرجية (AF-LER)
formulas.purpose_ar: توسع LER القياسي لتشمل منتجات الأشجار؛ AF-LER > 1 يؤكد ميزة استخدام الأرض.
formulas.variables_ar:
Ytree = محصول الخشب، الفاكهة، أو العلف من الأشجار
Stree = محصول الأشجار المزروعة بمفردها
اجمع كل المكونات الموجودة في النظام

formulas.name: Carbon Sequestration Rate (CSR)
formulas.formula: CSR = ΔC_biomass + ΔC_soil  [t CO₂-eq ha⁻¹ yr⁻¹]
formulas.variables:
ΔC_biomass = annual increment in tree + crop biomass C
ΔC_soil = annual change in soil organic C
formulas.purpose: Quantifies annual carbon sink of the agroforestry system for climate reporting.
formulas.example: Tree biomass C gain = 2.5 t/ha/yr, soil C gain = 0.3 t/ha/yr → CSR = 2.8 t C = 10.3 t CO₂-eq/ha/yr.
formulas.purpose_ar: يُقيس كمية الكربون المُخزنة سنويًا في نظام الزراعة الحرجية للتقارير المناخية.
formulas.variables_ar:
ΔC_biomass = الزيادة السنوية في الكربون الحيوي للأشجار + المحصول
ΔC_soil = التغير السنوي في الكربون العضوي في التربة

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Agroforestry is among the most effective land-based climate solutions — well-designed systems can sequester 2–10 t CO₂-eq/ha/yr while maintaining or improving food production.

chapters.title: Post-Harvest & Storage Metrics
chapters.intro: Post-harvest losses consume 30–40% of food production in many developing regions. Quantifying losses along the supply chain identifies intervention priorities and measures the effectiveness of storage improvements.

formulas.name: Weight Loss Percentage (WL%)
formulas.formula: WL% = (Initial weight − Final weight) / Initial weight × 100
formulas.variables:
Measured at specific intervals (7, 14, 30 days)
Include all loss pathways: respiration, moisture, pest damage
formulas.purpose: Most basic and universally used post-harvest loss metric.
formulas.example: Onions: 50 kg stored, 43 kg after 30 days → WL% = 14%.
formulas.pitfall: Distinguish physiological loss (normal) from pathological loss (preventable).
formulas.purpose_ar: أكثر مؤشر الفقد بعد الحصاد أساسية واستخدامًا عالميًا.
formulas.variables_ar:
يُقاس في فترات محددة (7، 14، 30 يوم)
يشمل جميع مسارات الفقد: التنفس، الرطوبة، أضرار الآفات

formulas.name: Respiration Rate (RR)
formulas.formula: RR = CO₂ evolved / (Weight × Time)  [mg CO₂ kg⁻¹ h⁻¹]
formulas.variables:
Higher RR = faster deterioration
Temperature doubles RR for every 10°C rise (Q₁₀ rule)
Used to predict shelf life
formulas.purpose: Measures metabolic rate of fresh produce; higher respiration = faster quality loss.
formulas.example: Strawberries at 20°C: 60 mg CO₂/kg/h (high) vs at 4°C: 12 mg CO₂/kg/h (manageable).

formulas.name: Q₁₀ Temperature Coefficient
formulas.formula: Q₁₀ = (RR at T+10) / (RR at T)  [dimensionless, typically 2–3]
formulas.variables:
Q₁₀ ≈ 2 for most fresh produce
Used to estimate storage life extension from cooling
formulas.purpose: Quantifies the acceleration of deterioration with temperature; basis for cold chain design.
formulas.example: Q₁₀=2.5: cooling from 25°C to 5°C → 2.5² = 6.25× longer shelf life.

formulas.name: Safe Storage Moisture Content
formulas.formula: EMC equilibrium charts: Grain moisture % where aw < 0.7 prevents mold
formulas.variables:
Maize: < 13.5% MC for long-term storage
Wheat: < 13.0%
Rice (paddy): < 14%
Legumes: < 12%
formulas.purpose: Critical threshold below which mold growth, mycotoxin production, and insect activity are suppressed.
formulas.example: Grain at 15% MC must be dried to 13.5% before hermetic storage to prevent aflatoxin.
formulas.pitfall: MC varies with temperature — use temperature-corrected charts.

formulas.name: Cold Chain Energy Efficiency (CCEE)
formulas.formula: CCEE = Tonnes of product stored × days / Energy consumed (kWh)
formulas.variables: Higher = more efficient cold storage
formulas.purpose: Evaluates energy cost of cold storage for economic and carbon footprint analysis.
formulas.example: 500 t apple store, 150 days, 12,000 kWh → CCEE = 6.25 t·day/kWh.

rows: Design modified atmosphere below critical RR
Dry to species-specific safe MC before storage

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Reducing post-harvest losses by 10% is equivalent to increasing production by 10% — with zero additional land, water, or input use.

chapters.title: Aquaculture Production Metrics
chapters.intro: Aquaculture is the world's fastest-growing food production sector, supplying over 50% of fish for human consumption. These metrics apply across finfish, shrimp, and bivalve culture systems in freshwater and marine environments.

formulas.name: Specific Growth Rate (SGR)
formulas.formula: SGR (% BW/day) = (ln W₂ − ln W₁) / (t₂ − t₁) × 100
formulas.variables:
W₁, W₂ = initial and final body weight (g)
t = days between weighings
Typical: tilapia 1.5–3.5%/day, shrimp 2–4%/day
formulas.purpose: Standard growth metric for aquaculture species; accounts for the exponential nature of fish growth.
formulas.example: Tilapia: W₁=50g, W₂=180g, 60 days → SGR = (ln180−ln50)/60×100 = 2.1%/day.
formulas.pitfall: Do not use simple ADG for aquaculture — SGR is the industry standard.
formulas.purpose_ar: قياس معدل النمو اليومي للأسماك كنسبة مئوية من وزن الجسم.

formulas.name: Feed Conversion Ratio (FCR – Aquaculture)
formulas.formula: FCR = Feed fed (dry) / Weight gain (wet)  [kg feed/kg fish]
formulas.variables:
Best practice: salmon 1.0–1.2; tilapia 1.3–1.8; shrimp 1.5–2.5; catfish 1.5–2.0
Must specify dry feed vs wet feed basis
formulas.purpose: Primary efficiency measure; lower FCR = less feed waste and lower nitrogen discharge.
formulas.example: Pond: 500 kg feed, 300 kg gain → FCR = 1.67.
formulas.pitfall: FCR worsens with poor water quality, disease, or feed quality — investigate root cause.
formulas.purpose_ar: كفاءة تحويل العلف الجاف إلى زيادة في الوزن الرطب للأسماك.

formulas.name: Survival Rate (SR)
formulas.variables:
Shrimp target: >70–80%
Tilapia cage: >90–95%
Salmon marine: >85–92%
formulas.purpose: Key indicator of husbandry success, disease management, and water quality control.
formulas.example: 50,000 shrimp stocked, 37,000 harvested → SR = 74%.
formulas.purpose_ar: نسبة الأسماك المحصودة إلى الأسماك المزرعة.

formulas.name: Stocking Density & Biomass
formulas.formula: Biomass (kg/m³) = Number of fish × Average weight (kg) / Volume (m³)
formulas.variables:
Tilapia RAS: 30–80 kg/m³
Salmon net pen: 15–30 kg/m³
Shrimp pond: 0.3–1.0 kg/m³
formulas.purpose: Current standing crop; used to determine feeding rate and predict oxygen demand.
formulas.example: 2000 tilapia × 0.25 kg / 10 m³ = 50 kg/m³ (at upper limit — monitor DO closely).
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب الكتلة الحيوية للماء بناءً على عدد الأسماك ووزنها.

formulas.name: Water Quality Index (WQI – Aquaculture)
formulas.formula: WQI = Σ(Wi × qi)  where Wi = weight and qi = quality score for each parameter
formulas.variables:
Parameters: DO, NH₃, NO₂, pH, Temperature, Turbidity, Alkalinity
DO critical threshold: >5 mg/L for most species
formulas.purpose: Composite index of water quality suitable for aquaculture production.
formulas.example: Monitor daily DO, weekly nutrients, monthly full WQI.
formulas.purpose_ar: مؤشر شامل لجودة المياه في أنظمة الاستزراع المائي.

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Aquaculture FCR of 1.2–1.5 for salmon compares favorably with beef (6–8) and pork (2.5–3.5) — aquaculture protein has among the lowest environmental footprints.

chapters.title: Pasture & Grazing Management
chapters.intro: Pasture is the critical interface between crop and animal production. Effective grazing management balances forage supply with livestock demand, maintaining plant productivity while maximizing animal performance and soil health.

formulas.name: Carrying Capacity (CC)
formulas.formula: CC = Forage available (kg DM/ha) / (Animal requirement kg DM/day × Grazing days)
formulas.variables:
Also expressed as Livestock Units (LSU) per hectare
1 LSU = 450 kg cow consuming 12 kg DM/day
Typical: semi-arid 0.3–0.8 LSU/ha; humid 2–4 LSU/ha
formulas.purpose: Maximum animal load a pasture can sustain without degradation.
formulas.example: Pasture: 3,500 kg DM/ha, 70% utilization = 2,450 kg available. One LSU needs 12 kg/day × 30 days = 360 kg. CC = 6.8 LSU/ha for one month.
formulas.pitfall: Never use full potential — target 60–70% utilization to maintain plant cover.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب الحد الأقصى للوحدات الحيوانية التي يمكن أن ترعى في مساحة معينة.

formulas.name: Pasture Growth Rate (PGR)
formulas.formula: PGR = (Dry matter at end − Dry matter at start) / Days  [kg DM ha⁻¹ day⁻¹]
formulas.variables:
Measured by excluding grazing in a cage
Highly seasonal — varies from 0 (drought) to 100+ kg/ha/day
formulas.purpose: Actual daily forage production rate; used to plan rotation timing and supplement programs.
formulas.example: Start: 1,200 kg DM/ha, End: 2,800 kg after 21 days → PGR = 76.2 kg DM/ha/day.
formulas.purpose_ar: معدل إنتاج العلف اليومي الفعلي؛ يُستخدم للتخطيط لتوقعات الدوران وبرامج المكملات الغذائية.
formulas.variables_ar:
يُقاس باستثناء الرعي في قفص
موسمي للغاية — يتراوح من 0 (جفاف) إلى 100+ كجم/هكتار/يوم

formulas.name: Stocking Rate (SR)
formulas.formula: SR = Number of animal units × Days grazed / Hectares grazed  [AU-days/ha]
formulas.variables:
AU = animal unit (450 kg beef cow equivalent)
Actual vs recommended SR ratio = grazing pressure
formulas.purpose: Expresses grazing intensity; compare to carrying capacity to assess over/understocking.
formulas.example: 50 cows (AU=1) on 25 ha for 120 days → SR = 50×120/25 = 240 AU-days/ha.
formulas.purpose_ar: يعبر عن شدة الرعي؛ يُقارن بالسعة التحملية لتقييم الزيادة أو النقص في الإشغال.
formulas.variables_ar:
وحدة الحيوان (AU) = وحدة حيوان مكافئة بقرة لحمية بوزن 450 كجم
نسبة معدل الإشغال الفعلي إلى الموصى به = ضغط الرعي

formulas.name: Rotational Grazing Rest Period (RP)
formulas.formula: RP = (Target leaf area recovery / PGR) × adjustment factor  [days]
formulas.variables:
Warm season grasses: 30–45 day rest
Cool season grasses: 20–35 days
Adjust by season and growth rate
formulas.purpose: Time required for pasture to recover adequate leaf area and root reserves before re-grazing.
formulas.example: Summer: PGR=60 kg/day, target 2500 kg regrowth → RP ≈ 42 days.
formulas.purpose_ar: الزمن المطلوب لاستعادة المراعي لمساحة أوراق كافية واحتياطات الجذر قبل إعادة الرعي.
formulas.variables_ar:
العشبات الموسمية الدافئة: راحة 30–45 يوم
العشبات الموسمية الباردة: 20–35 يوم
يُضبط حسب الموسم ومعدل النمو

formulas.name: Forage Utilization Efficiency (FUE)
formulas.formula: FUE (%) = Forage consumed by animals / Forage grown × 100
formulas.variables:
Target: 50–70% for rotational; 30–50% for continuous
Unutilized forage: trampled, rejected, or senesced
formulas.purpose: Efficiency of converting forage growth into animal product; key for system profitability.
formulas.example: PGR gives 4,000 kg DM/ha, animals consume 2,600 kg → FUE = 65%.
formulas.purpose_ar: كفاءة تحويل نمو العلف إلى منتج حيواني؛ مفتاح لربحية النظام.
formulas.variables_ar:
الهدف: 50–70% للدوران، 30–50% للدوري المستمر
العلف غير المستخدم: مُداس، مرفوض، أو متقدم في العمر

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Well-managed rotational grazing can sequester 0.5–1.5 t C/ha/yr in soils while maintaining or improving livestock productivity — one of the most accessible on-farm climate interventions.

chapters.title: Beekeeping & Apiculture Metrics
chapters.intro: Apiculture provides direct income from honey and other hive products, and indirect value through pollination services estimated at $235–577 billion in global crop value annually. These metrics support both commercial beekeeping and crop pollination management.

formulas.name: Colony Strength Index (CSI)
formulas.formula: CSI = (Frames of brood × 2) + Frames of bees + (Frames of stores × 0.5)
formulas.variables:
Score out of maximum possible for hive size
Healthy spring colony: CSI > 15 (10-frame hive)
Correlates with honey production potential
formulas.purpose: Rapid assessment of colony productive potential without disassembly.
formulas.example: 4 brood frames, 8 bee frames, 6 store frames → CSI = 8+8+3 = 19 (strong).
formulas.pitfall: Calibrate scoring to local race and season — Africanized colonies build faster.
formulas.purpose_ar: تقييم سريع لإمكانات إنتاجية المستعمرة دون تفكيكها.
formulas.variables_ar:
نتيجة من أصل الممكن لخلية النحل
مستعمرة صحية في الربيع: CSI > 15 (خلية 10 إطارات)
يرتبط بإمكانات إنتاج العسل

formulas.name: Varroa Mite Infestation Rate (VIR)
formulas.purpose: Primary indicator of varroa destructor infestation; key for treatment timing.
formulas.example: 9 mites in 300-bee sample → VIR = 3.0% (immediate treatment required).
formulas.pitfall: Sample from brood nest bees, not foragers — foragers underestimate infestation.
formulas.purpose_ar: المؤشر الرئيسي لإصابة القراد الفارو destructor؛ مفتاح لتوقيت العلاج.

formulas.name: Honey Yield per Colony (HY)
formulas.formula: HY = Honey harvested (kg) / Number of colonies  [kg/colony]
formulas.variables:
World average: 20–25 kg
Intensive managed: 40–80 kg
Depends heavily on forage area and season
formulas.purpose: Primary productivity measure for commercial beekeeping operations.
formulas.example: 600 kg extracted from 18 colonies → HY = 33.3 kg/colony.
formulas.purpose_ar: مقياس الإنتاجية الرئيسية لعمليات تربية النحل التجارية.
formulas.variables_ar:
المتوسط العالمي: 20–25 كجم
إدارة مكثفة: 40–80 كجم
يعتمد بشكل كبير على منطقة العلف والموسم

formulas.name: Pollination Service Index (PSI)
formulas.formula: PSI = Colony density × Foraging radius × Crop dependence ratio
formulas.variables:
Colony density = hives/ha
Foraging radius = effective range (typically 2 km)
Crop dependence: 0 (self-fertile) to 1 (fully dependent)
formulas.purpose: Estimates pollination service delivery to a target crop.
formulas.example: Almond (dependence=0.65): 2 hives/ha × effective 3 km range → high PSI.
formulas.purpose_ar: يُقدر تقديم خدمة التلقيح لمحصول مستهدف.
formulas.variables_ar:
الكثافة المستعمرة = خلايا/هكتار
نطاق التغذية = المدى الفعلي (عادة 2 كم)
اعتماد المحصول: 0 (ذاتي الإخصاب) إلى 1 (معتمد بالكامل)

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: A single honey bee colony pollinates crops worth $2,000–$20,000/year depending on crop type. Colony health directly determines both honey yield and pollination service value.

chapters.title: Animal Stress Physiology & Welfare Metrics
chapters.intro: Stress directly reduces feed intake, growth, reproduction, and immune function. Quantifying stress load allows producers to make evidence-based management decisions that improve both animal welfare and production efficiency.

formulas.name: Temperature-Humidity Index (THI)
formulas.variables:
T = dry-bulb temperature (°C)
RH = relative humidity (%)
Dairy cattle: >68 = mild stress; >72 = moderate; >78 = severe
formulas.purpose: Single index combining temperature and humidity effects on heat stress in cattle.
formulas.pitfall: THI thresholds differ by species: poultry stress begins at THI>75; pigs at THI>70.
formulas.purpose_ar: مؤشر واحد يجمع تأثير درجة الحرارة والرطوبة على الإجهاد الحراري في الماشية.
formulas.variables_ar:
T = درجة حرارة الطلق الجاف (°C)
RH = الرطوبة النسبية (%)
بقرة الألبان: >68 = إجهاد خفيف؛ >72 = معتدل؛ >78 = شديد

formulas.name: Respiration Rate (RR) as Heat Stress Proxy
formulas.formula: Normal: cattle 10–30 breaths/min; Mild stress: 40–60; Severe: >80
formulas.variables:
Non-invasive field measurement
Count flank movements for 15 seconds × 4
Most reliable stress indicator in absence of instruments
formulas.purpose: Rapid field assessment of heat stress severity without equipment.
formulas.example: Cow showing 72 breaths/min on hot day → severe heat stress, shade and water immediately.
formulas.purpose_ar: تقييم سريع في الميدان لشدة الإجهاد الحراري بدون معدات.
formulas.variables_ar:
قياس غير جراحي في الميدان
عدد حركات الجانب لمدة 15 ثانية × 4
مؤشر الإجهاد الأكثر موثوقية في غياب الأدوات

formulas.name: Body Condition Score Change Rate (BCSCR)
formulas.variables:
BCS loss > 1 point in first 60 days post-calving = high NEB risk
Target: < 0.5 BCS loss in transition period
formulas.purpose: Tracks rate of energy mobilization from body reserves; key transition management tool.
formulas.example: BCS drops from 3.5 to 2.8 in 45 days → BCSCR = −0.016/day (acceptable).
formulas.purpose_ar: يتتبع معدل تحطيم الطاقة من مخازن الجسم؛ أداة أساسية لإدارة فترة التحول.
formulas.variables_ar:
فقدان BCS > 1 نقطة في أول 60 يوم بعد الولادة = مخمر كبير لـ NEB
الهدف: < 0.5 فقدان BCS في فترة التحول

formulas.name: Animal Welfare Index (AWI)
formulas.formula: AWI = Σ(Domain score × Domain weight) / Total weight  [0–100 scale]
formulas.variables:
Domains: nutrition, environment, health, behavior, mental state
Based on Five Freedoms / Five Domains framework
Score > 80 = good welfare
formulas.purpose: Composite welfare assessment for certification, insurance, or improvement planning.
formulas.example: Score domains 1–5 on 0–20 scale each → sum / 100 for normalized AWI.
formulas.purpose_ar: تقييم شامل لرفاهية الحيوان للأ certificatio، التأمين، أو التخطيط للتحسين.
formulas.variables_ar:
المجالات: التغذية، البيئة، الصحة، السلوك، الحالة النفسية
مبني على إطار الأربعينيات / مجالات الخمس
النتيجة > 80 = رفاهية جيدة

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Reducing heat stress in dairy cattle by THI management can recover 10–25% of lost milk production — returning $100–300/cow/summer in affected regions.

chapters.title: AI & Machine Learning Model Metrics
chapters.intro: Machine learning models are increasingly used for crop yield prediction, disease detection, soil mapping, and livestock monitoring. Understanding model evaluation metrics is essential for critically interpreting AI tools in agricultural decision-making.

formulas.name: Root Mean Square Error (RMSE)
formulas.formula: RMSE = √(Σ(Predicted − Observed)² / n)  [same units as variable]
formulas.variables:
n = number of observations
Lower RMSE = better prediction accuracy
Compare to mean value: RMSE/mean < 10% = excellent
formulas.purpose: Most common accuracy metric for continuous predictions (yield, weight, milk production).
formulas.example: Yield model: 10 plots, RMSE = 0.45 t/ha vs mean yield 4.2 t/ha → RMSE% = 10.7% (acceptable).
formulas.pitfall: RMSE penalizes large errors more than small — better than MAE for outlier-sensitive applications.
formulas.purpose_ar: مؤشر الدقة الأكثر شيوعًا للتنبؤات المستمرة (المحصول، الوزن، إنتاج الحليب).
formulas.variables_ar:
n = عدد المشاهدات
RMSE أقل = دقة تنبؤ أفضل
المقارنة بالقيمة المتوسطة: RMSE/المتوسط < 10% = ممتاز

formulas.name: Mean Absolute Error (MAE)
formulas.formula: MAE = Σ|Predicted − Observed| / n  [same units as variable]
formulas.variables:
More interpretable than RMSE
Equally weights all errors
Compare RMSE/MAE: ratio > 1.2 suggests outliers present
formulas.purpose: Average prediction error in original units; easier to explain to non-technical stakeholders.
formulas.example: MAE = 0.3 t/ha for yield prediction → on average predictions are within 300 kg/ha.
formulas.purpose_ar: متوسط خطأ التنبؤ في الوحدات الأصلية؛ أسهل لشرحه لمستشارين غير تقنيين.
formulas.variables_ar:
أكثر قابلية للتفسير من RMSE
يعطي نفس الوزن لجميع الأخطاء
مقارنة RMSE/MAE: النسبة > 1.2 تشير إلى وجود قيم شاذة

formulas.name: Confusion Matrix Metrics (Classification)
formulas.variables:
TP=true positive, FP=false positive, FN=false negative
Disease detection: prioritize Recall (minimize missed cases)
Weed mapping: prioritize Precision (minimize false spraying)
formulas.purpose: Evaluates binary classification models (disease yes/no, pest present/absent).
formulas.example: Disease detector: Precision=0.88, Recall=0.91, F1=0.895 → good balanced performance.
formulas.pitfall: Accuracy alone is misleading with class imbalance (rare disease = high accuracy by always predicting "no disease").
formulas.purpose_ar: يقيّم نماذج التصنيف الثنائي (مرض نعم/لا، آفرة حاضر/غائب).
formulas.variables_ar:
TP=إيجابي صحيح، FP=إيجابي خاطئ، FN=سلبي خاطئ
كشف الأمراض: أولوية تذكر (تقليل الحالات المفقودة)
رسم خرائط الأعشاب: أولوية الدقة (تقليل الرش الخاطئ)

formulas.name: R² (Coefficient of Determination)
formulas.variables:
R² > 0.85 = good model fit for agricultural applications
R² can be negative for poorly fitted models
Always report alongside RMSE — R² alone misleads
formulas.purpose: Proportion of variation in the outcome explained by the model.
formulas.example: Yield prediction: R²=0.87 → model explains 87% of yield variation.
formulas.purpose_ar: النسبة المئوية للتغير في النتيجة المفسر بالنموذج.
formulas.variables_ar:
R² > 0.85 = ملاءمة جيدة للنموذج للتطبيقات الزراعية
يمكن أن تكون R² سالبة للنماذج غير الملائمة دائمًا
أبلغ دائمًا مع RMSE — R² وحدها تضليل

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: AI-powered variable-rate application guided by field-level models can reduce fertilizer use by 15–25% and pesticide use by 20–30% with maintained or improved yields.

chapters.title: Drone & UAV Mission Planning Formulas
chapters.intro: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are now routine tools for crop scouting, multispectral imaging, and precision spraying. Effective mission planning requires understanding coverage, resolution, and safety parameters.

formulas.name: Ground Sampling Distance (GSD)
formulas.formula: GSD (cm/px) = (Altitude m × Sensor width mm) / (Focal length mm × Image width px)
formulas.variables:
Lower GSD = higher resolution
Plant counting requires GSD < 2 cm
Canopy cover mapping: GSD < 5 cm sufficient
formulas.purpose: Defines the spatial resolution of captured imagery; drives altitude and flight time planning.
formulas.example: DJI Phantom 4 Multispectral at 120 m: GSD ≈ 5.3 cm — suitable for NDVI mapping.
formulas.pitfall: GSD doubles when altitude doubles — balance coverage speed vs resolution needed.
formulas.purpose_ar: يحدد الدقة المكانية للصور الملتقطة؛ يدفع تخطيط الارتفاع ووقت الطيران.
formulas.variables_ar:
GSD أقل = دقة أعلى
عد النباتات يتطلب GSD < 2 سم
رسم خرائط التغطية النباتية: GSD < 5 سم كافٍ

formulas.name: Flight Coverage Rate
formulas.variables:
Include turnaround time at field edges
Typical: 15–50 ha/hr for imaging; 5–10 ha/hr for spraying
formulas.purpose: Mission planning metric to estimate number of flights and battery requirements.
formulas.example: Speed=8 m/s, swath=60 m → Coverage = 8×60×3600/10000 = 172.8 ha/hr (imaging mode).
formulas.purpose_ar: مؤشر تخطيم المهمة لتقدير عدد الرحلات ومتطلبات البطارية.
formulas.variables_ar:
تضمين وقت الدوران عند حواف الحقل
المعتاد: 15–50 هكتار/ساعة للتصوير؛ 5–10 هكتار/ساعة للرش

formulas.name: Spray Drone Application Rate
formulas.formula: Application rate (L/ha) = Flow rate (L/min) × 60 / (Speed m/s × Swath m × 0.36)
formulas.variables:
Typical application rate: 10–30 L/ha for spray drones
Ultra-low volume (ULV): 1–3 L/ha for specialized formulations
formulas.purpose: Ensures correct pesticide dosage when using spray drones.
formulas.example: Flow=1.2 L/min, speed=4 m/s, swath=4 m → Rate = 72/5.76 = 12.5 L/ha.
formulas.pitfall: Calibrate flow meter before each field — temperature affects nozzle output.
formulas.name_ar: معدل تطبيق رش الطائرات بدون طيار
formulas.purpose_ar: يضمن الجرعة الصحيحة من المبيدات عند استخدام طائرات رش بدون طيار.
formulas.variables_ar:
معدل التطبيق المعتاد: 10–30 لتر/هكتار لطائرات الرش
حجم منخفض جدًا (ULV): 1–3 لتر/هكتار للصيغ المتخصصة

formulas.name: Image Overlap for Orthomosaic
formulas.variables:
Higher overlap = better photogrammetry accuracy but more images and processing time
80/70 overlap standard for agricultural mapping
Windy conditions require higher overlap (85/80)
formulas.purpose: Ensures sufficient image pairs for photogrammetric reconstruction of orthomap.
formulas.example: Field with 85/75 overlap at 5 m/s → approximately 3× more images than 70/60 overlap.
formulas.purpose_ar: يضمن أزواج صور كافية لإعادة بناء المثلثات الضوئية للمصفوفة الأرضية.
formulas.variables_ar:
تداخل أعلى = دقة أفضل للمثلثات الضوئية ولكن المزيد من الصور ومعالجة وقت
80/70 تدليل معياري لرسم الخرائط الزراعية
ظروف الرياح تتطلب تداخلًا أعلى (85/80)

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Precision spraying drones guided by NDVI maps can reduce pesticide application by 30–50% through targeted application to problem zones only.

chapters.title: IoT Sensors & Data Quality Metrics
chapters.intro: Agricultural IoT sensors generate continuous streams of field data. Understanding data quality, calibration, and validation metrics is essential for making reliable decisions from sensor-derived measurements.

formulas.name: Sensor Accuracy & Drift
formulas.formula: Drift rate = (Reading at time t − Initial calibrated value) / Elapsed days  [units/day]
formulas.variables:
Cross-validate against manual measurements monthly
Temperature sensors: acceptable drift < 0.1°C/month
Soil EC sensors: recalibrate when drift >5% detected
formulas.purpose: Quantifies systematic sensor error accumulation over time.
formulas.example: Soil moisture sensor reads 32%, manual gravimetric = 29% → Error = 3%, consider recalibration.
formulas.purpose_ar: يقيس تراكم الخطأ المنهجي للمستشعِر بمرور الوقت.
formulas.variables_ar:
التحقق المتقاطع مع القياسات اليدوية شهرياً
مستشعرات الحرارة: الانحراف المقبول < 0.1°C/شهر
مستشعرات توصيلية التربة (EC): المعايرة مرة أخرى عند اكتشاف انحراف >5%

formulas.name: Data Completeness Index (DCI)
formulas.variables:
Target: >95% for critical sensors
<80% indicates connectivity or power problem
Weight by sensor importance for composite DCI
formulas.purpose: Measures reliability of sensor data transmission; critical for systems using sensor data for automation.
formulas.example: Weather station sends 95 of expected 96 hourly records → DCI = 99.0%.
formulas.purpose_ar: يقيس موثوقية نقل بيانات المستشعِر؛ حاسم للأنظمة التي تستخدم بيانات المستشعِر للتحكم الآلي.
formulas.variables_ar:
الهدف: >95% للمستشعرات الحرجة
<80% يشير إلى مشاكل الاتصال أو الطاقة
التسوية حسب أهمية المستشعِر لمؤشر DCI المركب

formulas.name: Alert Threshold Setting
formulas.formula: Alert Z-score = (Observation − Rolling mean) / Rolling SD
formulas.variables:
Z > 2.5 = unusual reading (alert)
Z > 3.5 = sensor fault likely
Window: 7–30 days rolling baseline
formulas.purpose: Statistical approach to distinguish genuine anomalies from sensor noise.
formulas.example: Soil moisture drops to Z=3.1 → genuine irrigation need or sensor disconnection — investigate.
formulas.purpose_ar: نهج إحصائي للتمييز بين الشذوذ الحقيقي والضوضاء في المستشعِر.
formulas.variables_ar:
Z > 2.5 = قراءة غير عادية (تنبيه)
Z > 3.5 = عطل في المستشعِر على الأرجح
النافذة: خط أساس متحرك لمدة 7–30 يوم

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: High-quality sensor data can reduce over-irrigation by 20–30% and enable precise fertilization — but only if data completeness and calibration are actively managed.

chapters.title: Carbon Markets & MRV Metrics
chapters.intro: Measurement, Reporting, and Verification (MRV) frameworks enable farmers to generate and sell carbon credits. Understanding these calculations is increasingly important as voluntary carbon markets expand in agriculture.

formulas.name: Soil Carbon Stock (SCS)
formulas.variables:
Multiply by 3.67 to convert C to CO₂-equivalent
Measure in 0–30 cm and 30–100 cm layers separately
formulas.purpose: Baseline measurement for soil carbon projects; repeated sampling detects change over time.
formulas.pitfall: Ensure same BD sampling method and depth at baseline and monitoring — inconsistency is largest source of error.
formulas.purpose_ar: قياس أساس لمشاريع الكربون في التربة؛ يتم أخذ العينات المتكرر لرصد التغير بمرور الوقت.
formulas.variables_ar:
اضرب في 3.67 لتحويل C إلى مكافئ CO₂
قياس طبقات 0–30 سم و 30–100 سم بشكل منفصل

formulas.name: Annual Soil Carbon Change
formulas.variables:
Minimum 2–3 years monitoring before crediting
Typical creditable change: 0.3–1.5 t CO₂-eq/ha/yr for improved management
formulas.purpose: Annual carbon sequestration rate eligible for carbon credit generation.
formulas.example: SCS change: +2.1 t C/ha over 3 years → ΔC = 0.70 t C/ha/yr = 2.57 t CO₂-eq/ha/yr.
formulas.purpose_ar: معدل تخزين الكربون السنوي المؤهل لإنشاء ائتمان الكربون.
formulas.variables_ar:
الحد الأدنى 2–3 سنوات من المراقبة قبل الائتمان
التغير المؤهل للائتمان: 0.3–1.5 طن CO₂-مكاف/هكتار/سنة للإدارة المحسنة

formulas.name: N₂O Emissions from Fertilizer (IPCC Tier 1)
formulas.formula: N₂O (kg N/ha) = N_applied × EF1  where EF1 = 0.01 (1% default)
formulas.variables:
Convert to CO₂-eq: × 298 (GWP of N₂O)
Accounts for direct emissions only; add indirect (leaching, volatilization)
EF1 varies 0.003–0.03 regionally
formulas.purpose: Default method for estimating direct N₂O emissions from synthetic and organic N applications.
formulas.example: 150 kg N/ha urea → N₂O = 1.5 kg N/ha = 2.4 kg N₂O/ha = 711 kg CO₂-eq/ha.
formulas.purpose_ar: الطريقة الافتراضية لتقدير انبعاثات N₂O المباشرة من تطبيقات الن العضوي والاصطناعي.
formulas.variables_ar:
تحويل إلى CO₂-مكاف: × 298 (قيمة الاحتباس الحراري لـ N₂O)
يأخذ في الاعتبار الانبعاثات المباشرة فقط؛ أضف الانبعاثات غير المباشرة (التصريف، التطاير)
EF1 يتراوح 0.003–0.03 حسب المنطقة

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Farmers in verified carbon programs receive $15–80/t CO₂-eq — a 1 t/ha/yr improvement across 50 ha generates $750–4,000/year additional income with no additional inputs.

chapters.title: Supply Chain & Value Chain Analysis
chapters.intro: Understanding where value is created and captured along the agricultural value chain reveals opportunities for smallholder income improvement, investment, and policy intervention.

formulas.name: Marketing Margin (MM)
formulas.formula: MM = Consumer price − Farm-gate price  [per unit or %]
formulas.variables:
MM% = (Consumer price − Farm price) / Consumer price × 100
Identify which chain actors capture most value
formulas.purpose: Measures the share of final price that does not return to the farmer.
formulas.example: Consumer pays $2.50/kg tomato; farmer receives $0.60/kg → MM% = 76% captured by chain.
formulas.purpose_ar: يقيس حظيرة السعر النهائي التي لا تعود إلى المزارع.
formulas.variables_ar:
MM% = (سعر المستهلك − سعر المزرعة) / سعر المستهلك × 100
تحديد أي من المشاركين في السلسلة يمثل أكبر قيمة

formulas.name: Farm-Gate Price Ratio (FGPR)
formulas.variables:
FGPR < 1 = farmer receiving below reference (market distortion or inefficiency)
FGPR > 1 = premium (quality, certification, proximity)
formulas.purpose: Benchmarks local farm prices against reference prices to identify market failures.
formulas.example: Maize farm gate: $180/t vs regional reference $220/t → FGPR = 0.82 (18% below reference).
formulas.purpose_ar: يحدد أسعار المزارع المحلية مقابل الأسعار المرجعية لتحديد حالات فشل السوق.
formulas.variables_ar:
FGPR < 1 = المزارع يتقاضي أقل من المرجع (تشويه السوق أو عدم الكفاءة)
FGPR > 1 = متميز (جودة، شهادة، قرب)

formulas.name: Value Chain Node Profitability
formulas.formula: Node margin = Selling price − Buying price − Processing/transport costs  [per unit]
formulas.variables:
Apply to each node: farm → collection → processor → retailer
Identifies bottlenecks and opportunities for vertical integration
formulas.purpose: Decomposes value chain margins by actor to reveal distribution of profit.
formulas.example: Collector buys at $0.60, sells at $0.90, transport $0.10/kg → Node margin = $0.20/kg.
formulas.purpose_ar: يفكك هوامش سلسلة القيمة حسب المشارك ليكشف عن توزيع الربح.
formulas.variables_ar:
تطبيق على كل عقد: مزرعة → تجميع → معالج → تجزئة
تحديد الاختناقات والفرص للتكامل الرأسي

formulas.name: Price Transmission Coefficient (PTC)
formulas.variables:
PTC = 1 = perfect transmission (competitive market)
PTC < 0.5 = poor farm price linkage (market power problem)
Estimated via regression of price time series
formulas.purpose: Measures how price changes at consumer level are passed back to farmers.
formulas.example: Consumer prices rose 20%, farm prices rose 8% → PTC = 0.40 (farmers poorly connected).
formulas.purpose_ar: يقيس كيفية انتقال تغيرات السعر للمستهلكين إلى المزارعين.
formulas.variables_ar:
PTC = 1 = انتقال مثالي (سوق تنافسي)
PTC < 0.5 = ارتباط ضعيف بسعر المزرعة (مشكلة قوة السوق)
يُقدر عبر انحدار سلاسل زمنية للأسعار

chapters.title: Agricultural Risk & Insurance Metrics
chapters.intro: Agriculture is inherently risky. Quantifying production and price risk is essential for farm planning, lending decisions, insurance product design, and government safety net programs.

formulas.name: Coefficient of Variation (CV)
formulas.formula: CV (%) = (Standard deviation of yield / Mean yield) × 100
formulas.variables:
CV < 15% = low risk crop/variety
CV 15–25% = moderate risk
CV > 25% = high risk — consider insurance or diversification
formulas.purpose: Measures yield variability relative to the mean; primary risk assessment metric.
formulas.example: 10-year maize: mean=4.2 t/ha, SD=0.84 → CV = 20% (moderate variability).
formulas.pitfall: Use at least 10 years of data for reliable CV estimation.
formulas.purpose_ar: يقيس تغيرية المحصول بالنسبة للمتوسط؛ مقياس أساسي لتقييم المخاطر.
formulas.variables_ar:
CV < 15% = محصول/صنف منخفض المخاطر
CV 15–25% = مخاطر متوسطة
CV > 25% = مخاطر عالية — التفكير في التأمين أو التنويع

formulas.name: Value at Risk (VaR)
formulas.variables:
Assumes normal distribution of yields
1.645 SD = 95th percentile loss threshold
Reports worst expected yield in 1-in-20 years
formulas.purpose: Worst-case yield in a 1-in-20 year scenario; key for lender and insurer risk assessment.
formulas.example: Mean=4.2, SD=0.84 → VaR(95%) = 4.2−1.38 = 2.82 t/ha (worst 5% of years).
formulas.purpose_ar: أسوأ حالة لمحصول في سيناريو واحد من بين عشرين عامًا؛ مفتاح لتقييم المخاطر من قبل المقرضين والمؤمنين.
formulas.variables_ar:
يفترض توزيع طبيعي للمحاصيل
1.645 الانحراف المعياري = عتبة الخسارة في المئوية 95
يبلغ عن أسوأ محصول متوقع في حالة من بين عشرين عامًا

formulas.name: Insurance Premium Rate
formulas.formula: Premium rate (%) = Expected loss / Sum insured × Loading factor
formulas.variables:
Expected loss = mean shortfall below threshold × probability
Loading factor: 1.1–1.4 (insurer expenses + profit)
Index insurance: uses rainfall or NDVI trigger not actual loss
formulas.purpose: Actuarially fair premium basis for crop insurance product design.
formulas.example: Expected loss = $200/ha, sum insured = $1,000/ha, loading 1.2 → Premium = 24%.
formulas.purpose_ar: أسعار أقساط عادلة تأمينياً لمنتجات تأمين المحاصيل.
formulas.variables_ar:
الخسارة المتوقعة = متوسط النقص عن الحد الأدنى × الاحتمال
عامل التحميل: 1.1–1.4 (النفقات الربحية + أرباح شركة التأمين)
التأمين المؤشر: يستخدم محفزات الأمطار أو NDVI وليس الخسارة الفعلية

formulas.name: Basis Risk (Index Insurance)
formulas.formula: Basis risk = Correlation between index trigger and actual loss  [R²]
formulas.variables:
R² > 0.70 = acceptable basis risk for index insurance
High basis risk = farmers experience loss but do not receive payout
formulas.purpose: Key metric for evaluating index-based insurance product quality.
formulas.example: Rainfall index vs yield loss R²=0.62 → moderate basis risk, consider spatial refinement.
formulas.purpose_ar: مقياس أساسي لتقييم جودة منتجات التأمين القائمة على المؤشرات.
formulas.variables_ar:
R² > 0.70 = مخاطر أساسية مقبولة للتأمين المؤشر
مخاطر أساسية عالية = المزارعون يواجهون خسائر ولكنهم لا يحصلون على تعويض

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Well-designed agricultural insurance can increase smallholder adoption of improved inputs by 20–40% — risk protection enables investment in productivity.

chapters.title: Water Economics & Valuation
chapters.intro: As water scarcity intensifies, economic metrics are needed to allocate water efficiently, set prices, and evaluate investments in water-saving technologies.

formulas.name: Economic Water Productivity (EWP)
formulas.formula: EWP = Gross value of production / Volume of water consumed  [$/m³]
formulas.variables: Includes both irrigation and effective rainfall
formulas.purpose: Links water use to economic output; guides crop selection in water-limited environments.
formulas.example: Tomato: $3,000/ha value, 4,500 m³/ha water → EWP = $0.67/m³.
formulas.purpose_ar: يربط استخدام المياه بالنتاج الاقتصادي؛ يوجه اختيار المحاصيل في البيئات المحدودة بالمياه.
formulas.variables_ar: يشمل الري وهطول الأمطار الفعال

formulas.name: Shadow Price of Water (SPW)
formulas.formula: SPW = Marginal increase in profit / Marginal increase in water  [$/m³]
formulas.variables:
Derived from linear programming or production function estimation
Represents true economic value of water in production
formulas.purpose: Reflects opportunity cost of water; used for pricing and allocation decisions.
formulas.example: Profit increases $400/ha with additional 800 m³ irrigation → SPW = $0.50/m³.
formulas.purpose_ar: يعكس التكلفة الفرصة البديلة للمياه؛ تُستخدم لاتخاذ قرارات التسعية والتخصيص.
formulas.variables_ar:
مشتق من البرمجة الخطية أو تقدير دالة الإنتاج
يمثل القيمة الاقتصادية الحقيقية للمياه في الإنتاج

formulas.name: Groundwater Depletion Rate (GDR)
formulas.formula: GDR = Extraction rate − Recharge rate  [m³/yr or mm/yr]
formulas.variables:
Positive GDR = unsustainable extraction
Monitor water table depth over time
Aquifer-specific safe yield estimates needed
formulas.purpose: Identifies mining of groundwater beyond sustainable yield; critical for long-term planning.
formulas.example: Extraction 250 mm/yr, recharge 80 mm/yr → GDR = 170 mm/yr depletion.
formulas.purpose_ar: يحدد استنزاف المياه الجوفية ما دون الإنتاج المستدام؛ حاسم للتخطيط طويل الأمد.
formulas.variables_ar:
GDR موجب = استخلاص غير مستدام
مراقبة عمق مستوى المياه الجوفية بمرور الوقت
تتقدرات الإنتاج الآمن المحددة للمخزن المائي ضرورية

chapters.title: Gender, Equity & Social Metrics
chapters.intro: Agricultural development outcomes depend on who has access to resources and decision-making. Gender and social metrics quantify inclusion, equity, and empowerment in farming systems.

formulas.name: Women's Empowerment in Agriculture Index (WEAI)
formulas.variables:
5DE = 5 Domains of Empowerment score (0–1)
GPI = Gender Parity Index
Score > 0.8 = empowered; < 0.6 = disempowered
formulas.purpose: USAID/IFPRI tool measuring women's agency, resources, and leadership in agriculture.
formulas.example: Five domains: production decisions, resources, income, leadership, time allocation.
formulas.purpose_ar: أداة USAID/IFPRI لقياس الوكالة والموارد والقيادة للمرأة في الزراعة.
formulas.variables_ar:
5DE = درجة مجالات التمكين الخمسة (0–1)
GPI = مؤشر المساواة بين الجنسين
النتيجة > 0.8 = متمكنة؛ < 0.6 = غير متمكنة

formulas.name: Labor Productivity
formulas.formula: Labor productivity = Gross output value / Total labor hours (paid + unpaid)
formulas.variables:
Disaggregate by gender, age, task type
Compare across farm types and regions
formulas.purpose: Measures output per labor unit; reveals efficiency gaps and unpaid labor burdens.
formulas.example: Farm output $5,000, 800 labor hours → LP = $6.25/hr.
formulas.pitfall: Capture unpaid family labor in time-use surveys — commonly underestimated by 40–60%.
formulas.purpose_ar: يقيس النواتج لكل وحدة عمل؛ يكشف الفجوات الكفاءة والأعباء غير المدفوعة الأجر للعمل.
formulas.variables_ar:
تفكيك حسب الجنس والعمر ونوع المهمة
المقارنة بين أنواع المزارع والمناطق

formulas.name: Household Food Security Score (HFSS)
formulas.formula: HFSS = Σ(Dietary diversity score + Food access score + Stability score) / 3
formulas.variables:
Based on FIES (Food Insecurity Experience Scale) or HDDS (Household Dietary Diversity Score)
Scores: 1–8 food groups = better diversity
FIES severe: < 3 groups regularly consumed
formulas.purpose: Composite household food security and nutrition adequacy assessment.
formulas.example: Score 4 food groups regularly → moderate food security.
formulas.purpose_ar: تقييم مركب للأمن الغذائي والتغذية المنزلية.
formulas.variables_ar:
يعتمد على FIES (مقياس تجربة انعدام الأمن الغذائي) أو HDDS (مقياس التنوع الغذائي المنزلي)
النتائج: 1–8 مجموعات غذائية = تنوع أفضل
FIE شديد: < 3 مجموعات استهلكت بانتظام

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Closing the gender gap in agricultural productivity (access to inputs, extension, land) could increase yields by 20–30% among women farmers — equivalent to lifting 100–150 million people from hunger.

chapters.title: Comprehensive Conversion Tables
chapters.intro: Quick Reference

chapters.title: Master Formula Index
chapters.intro:
Complete alphabetical index of all formulas, indices, and metrics in this handbook with chapter reference.
Quick Reference

chapters.title: Printable Formula Quick-Reference Cards
chapters.intro: The following pages contain standalone formula cards — one per key metric — designed for printing, laminating, and use in field training, extension workshops, and farm management. Each card includes the formula, variables, a worked example, and the critical pitfall to avoid.

rows: PP = 10,000 / (Row spacing m × Plant spacing m)
Variables: Spacing in meters. Output: plants/ha.
⚠  Pitfall: Mixing cm and m units
⚠  Pitfall: Using % as whole number instead of decimal
Variables: Yields in same units. Output: %.
Example: Grain 5 t/ha, Biomass 12 t/ha → HI = 41.7%
⚠  Pitfall: Using fresh weight instead of dry weight
Variables: Yi = intercrop yield, Ys = sole crop yield.
⚠  Pitfall: Comparing to different-season sole crops
Variables: Water = total ET or irrigation applied.
⚠  Pitfall: Using irrigation only, ignoring rainfall
Variables: Both must be on same moisture basis.
Example: Lamb: 40 kg feed ÷ 10 kg gain = FCR 4.0
⚠  Pitfall: Mixing as-fed and dry matter basis
Variables: Weigh after overnight fast at same time of day.
Variables: M=kg milk, F=kg fat, P=kg protein (not %).
⚠  Pitfall: Using % directly without converting to kg
Variables: NIR and Red = reflectance values (0–1).
⚠  Pitfall: Saturates at LAI>3; use EVI for dense canopies
