Variables: Sum daily across season. Tbase is crop-specific.
⚠  Pitfall: Using wrong Tbase for the crop species
Variables: N rate in kg/ha, Nutrient % from bag label.
Example: 120 kg N/ha, Urea 46% N → 261 kg urea/ha
⚠  Pitfall: Confusing P element with P₂O₅ oxide form
⚠  Pitfall: Using disturbed samples — always use cores

chapters.title: Salinity & Salt-Affected Soil Management
chapters.intro: More than 250 million hectares of agricultural land globally are affected by salinity, with an additional 1–2 million ha lost each year to secondary salinization from irrigation. These metrics guide reclamation planning, crop selection, and long-term salt balance management.

formulas.name: Leaching Requirement for Reclamation (LR)
formulas.variables:
ECiw = EC of irrigation water (dS/m)
ECe_threshold = maximum tolerable soil EC for crop (dS/m)
LR expressed as fraction of irrigation water that must drain
formulas.purpose: Fraction of applied water needed to leach salt below root zone to achieve target ECe.
formulas.example: ECiw=2.0 dS/m, ECe threshold for wheat=6.0: LR = 2/(30−2) = 0.071 (7.1% of applied water must drain).
formulas.pitfall: Does not account for rainfall contribution to leaching — subtract effective rainfall from leaching need.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب كمية المياه الإضافية اللازمة لغسيل الأملاح من التربة.

formulas.name: Gypsum Requirement (GR)
formulas.variables:
ESP = exchangeable sodium percentage (%)
CEC = cation exchange capacity (cmol/kg)
BD = bulk density (g/cm³), depth in cm
1.72 = conversion factor for CaSO₄·2H₂O purity
formulas.purpose: Tonnes of gypsum needed to replace sodium on exchange sites to reach target ESP.
formulas.example: ESP_i=25%, ESP_t=10%, CEC=20, BD=1.3, depth=30cm → GR ≈ 7.2 t/ha.
formulas.pitfall: Account for gypsum purity (commercial grades: 70–95% CaSO₄·2H₂O).
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب كمية الجبس اللازمة لاستصلاح الترب الصودية.

formulas.name: Relative Yield under Salinity (Threshold-Slope Model)
formulas.formula: RY (%) = 100 − b × (ECe − ECe_threshold)  when ECe > threshold
formulas.variables:
ECe_threshold = salinity at which yield begins to decline (dS/m)
b = slope (% yield loss per dS/m above threshold)
RY = 100% below threshold; decreases linearly above
formulas.purpose: Van Genuchten-Hoffman model predicting crop yield loss from soil salinity.
formulas.pitfall: Threshold and slope values are crop- and cultivar-specific — use published tables.
formulas.purpose_ar: تقدير الإنتاج النسبي للمحاصيل تحت ظروف الملوحة.

formulas.name: Salt Balance Equation
formulas.variables:
Salt_in = ECiw × Volume applied × 0.64 (converts dS/m·m³ to kg salt)
Target: ΔSalt ≤ 0 for sustainable irrigated agriculture
Monitor seasonal salt balance; reclamation needed if positive
formulas.purpose: Annual audit of salt inputs vs outputs in an irrigated field.
formulas.example: ECiw=1.5 dS/m, 6000 m³/ha applied → Salt_in = 5,760 kg/ha. Must drain ≥5,760 kg to balance.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب ميزان الأملاح في التربة بين المدخلات والمخرجات.

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Irrigating with high-ECw water without adequate drainage is the leading cause of land degradation in arid zones — salt balance audits should be annual practice.

chapters.title: Cover Crop & Crop Rotation Metrics
chapters.intro: Cover crops and diverse rotations are foundational to regenerative agriculture. These metrics quantify their contribution to soil health, weed suppression, nitrogen fixation, and cropping system productivity.

formulas.name: Nitrogen Fixation Credit (NFC)
formulas.formula: NFC (kg N/ha) = Biomass DM × N% × Ndfa fraction
formulas.variables:
Ndfa = proportion of N derived from atmosphere (0.6–0.9 for well-nodulated legumes)
Biomass N from lab analysis or default values
Credit available to following cash crop after decomposition
formulas.purpose: Nitrogen contribution of legume cover crop to the following crop, reducing synthetic N need.
formulas.example: Vetch: 4 t DM/ha, 3.5% N, Ndfa=0.75 → NFC = 4×35×0.75 = 105 kg N/ha.
formulas.pitfall: Only a fraction (30–60%) mineralizes in the same season — adjust timing accordingly.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب كمية النيتروجين التي تثبتها المحاصيل البقولية.

formulas.name: Biomass Production Index (BPI)
formulas.formula: BPI = Cover crop DM (kg/ha) / Days of growth
formulas.variables:
Higher BPI = faster biomass accumulation
Compare species and management options
Minimum: 2,000 kg DM/ha for effective soil coverage
formulas.purpose: Productivity rate of cover crop; used to compare species and optimize termination timing.
formulas.example: Cereal rye: 3,800 kg DM in 90 days → BPI = 42.2 kg/ha/day.
formulas.purpose_ar: قياس معدل إنتاج الكتلة الحيوية اليومي للمحاصيل الغطائية.

formulas.name: Rotation Diversity Index (RDI)
formulas.formula: RDI = 1 − Σ(pi²)  where pi = proportion of rotation occupied by crop i
formulas.variables:
Adapted from Simpson diversity index
RDI = 0 (monoculture) to approaching 1 (maximum diversity)
Minimum RDI of 0.5 recommended for sustainable rotations
formulas.purpose: Measures crop sequence diversity; higher RDI associated with lower disease pressure and better soil health.
formulas.purpose_ar: قياس تنوع المحاصيل في الدورة الزراعية.

formulas.name: Soil Cover Percentage (SCP)
formulas.formula: SCP (%) = Covered area / Total area × 100  (measured by point-quadrat or image analysis)
formulas.variables:
Target: >70% during critical rainfall erosion periods
Below 30% = unprotected soil, high erosion risk
formulas.purpose: Proportion of soil surface protected by living or dead mulch; key erosion protection metric.
formulas.example: Count 50 points: 38 covered → SCP = 76% (adequate protection).
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب نسبة المساحة المغطاة بالغطاء النباتي.

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Diverse rotations with legume cover crops can replace 50–150 kg synthetic N/ha/yr, reducing both input costs and N₂O emissions substantially.

chapters.title: Seed Technology & Vigour Metrics
chapters.intro: Seed quality determines the foundation of every crop. Vigour testing goes beyond standard germination to predict field emergence under stress — the conditions that matter most.

formulas.name: Germination Index (GI)
formulas.formula: GI = Σ(Gt / Dt)  where Gt = seeds germinated on day t, Dt = days from start
formulas.variables:
Higher GI = faster, more uniform germination
Sensitive to seed lot differences that standard germination misses
formulas.purpose: Combines speed and uniformity of germination into a single index.
formulas.purpose_ar: قياس سرعة وانتظام إنبات البذور.

formulas.name: Seedling Vigour Index (SVI)
formulas.formula: SVI = (Shoot length + Root length) mm × Germination %  / 100
formulas.variables:
Measured at end of standard germination test (7–10 days)
SVI > 1500 = high vigour; < 500 = poor vigour
formulas.purpose: Combines seedling size and germination rate; predicts field emergence better than germination alone.
formulas.example: Shoot=65mm, Root=80mm, Germination=88% → SVI = 145×0.88 = 127.6 (moderate).
formulas.pitfall: Standardize growth media, temperature, and measurement method between comparisons.
formulas.purpose_ar: تقييم قوة بادرات البذور بناءً على الطول ونسبة الإنبات.

formulas.name: Mean Germination Time (MGT)
formulas.variables:
ti = time in days
ni = number germinating on day ti
Lower MGT = faster germination
formulas.purpose: Average time to germination; lower is better for field crop establishment.
formulas.example: Seeds germinate on days 3,4,5: 20,50,30 seeds → MGT=(3×20+4×50+5×30)/100 = 4.1 days.
formulas.purpose_ar: متوسط وقت الإنبات؛ الأقل هو الأفضل لتأسيس المحصول في الحقل.
formulas.variables_ar:
ti = الوقت بالأيام
ni = عدد البذور المنبتة في اليوم ti
MGT الأقل = إنبات أسرع

formulas.name: Emergence Rate Index (ERI)
formulas.formula: ERI = Σ(ni × k / ti)  where k = number of counting periods
formulas.variables:
Similar to GI but field-based
Higher ERI = faster field establishment
formulas.purpose: Field version of germination index; measures speed of crop emergence under actual conditions.
formulas.example: Count at 7, 10, 14 days post-sowing with k=3 counting periods.
formulas.purpose_ar: نسخة حقلية لمؤشر الإنبات؛ يقيس سرعة ظهور المحصول في الظروف الفعلية.
formulas.variables_ar:
مشابه لـ GI لكنه قائم على الحقل
مؤشر ERI الأعلى = تأسيس أسرع في الحقل

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Using certified high-vigour seed can improve field emergence by 15–25% compared to farm-saved seed, often returning more value than the premium cost.

chapters.title: Crop Simulation & Modelling Fundamentals
chapters.intro: Crop simulation models (DSSAT, APSIM, AquaCrop, WOFOST) are increasingly used in research, breeding, and policy. Understanding their key parameters and outputs allows practitioners to interpret and apply model results critically.

formulas.name: Radiation Use Efficiency (RUE)
formulas.formula: RUE = Aboveground biomass / Intercepted PAR  [g DM / MJ]
formulas.variables:
PAR = photosynthetically active radiation (400–700 nm)
Intercepted PAR = incoming PAR × (1 − e^(−k × LAI))
Typical values: wheat 1.2–1.8, maize 1.5–2.0, soybean 0.9–1.4 g/MJ
formulas.purpose: Core efficiency parameter in radiation-driven crop models; varies by species, N status, and water stress.
formulas.example: Maize: 15 t DM/ha over season, intercepted PAR = 900 MJ/m² → RUE = 150/900 = 1.67 g/MJ.
formulas.pitfall: RUE declines under water, N, or heat stress — use as a diagnostic of limiting factors.
formulas.purpose_ar: معامل الكفاءة الأساسي في نماذج المحصول المدفوعة بالإشعاع؛ يختلف حسب النوع، وحالة النيتروجين، وضغط الماء.
formulas.variables_ar:
PAR = الإشعاع الفوتوسينثي الفعال (400–700 نانومتر)
PAR المعترض = PAR الوارد × (1 − e^(−k × LAI))
القيم النموذجية: القمح 1.2–1.8، الذرة 1.5–2.0، فول الصويا 0.9–1.4 غ/مج

formulas.name: Transpiration Efficiency Coefficient (TEC)
formulas.formula: TE = Biomass produced / Water transpired  [g DM / kg H₂O]  or  [kg / mm]
formulas.variables:
TEC relates to vapour pressure deficit: TE × VPD ≈ constant for a species
C4 crops (maize): TEC ≈ 8–12; C3 crops (wheat): TEC ≈ 4–8
formulas.purpose: Links water use to biomass production; used in water-limited model simulations.
formulas.example: Wheat: 8 t DM/ha, 300 mm transpired → TE = 80/300 = 0.27 kg DM/mm.
formulas.purpose_ar: يربط استخدام الماء بالإنتاج البيولوجي؛ يُستخدم في محاكيات النماذج المحدودة بالماء.
formulas.variables_ar:
TEC يتعلق بعجز ضغط البخار: TE × VPD ≈ ثابت لنوع معين
محاصيل C4 (الذرة): TEC ≈ 8–12؛ محاصيل C3 (القمح): TEC ≈ 4–8

formulas.name: Yield Gap Analysis
formulas.formula: Yield gap = Potential yield − Actual yield  [t/ha]
formulas.variables:
Potential yield = water + nutrient unlimited, pest free (radiation limited only)
Water-limited yield = nutrient unlimited, pest free
Actual yield = farmer field average
formulas.purpose: Decomposes the gap between what farmers achieve and what is physically possible.
formulas.example: Wheat: potential=10 t/ha, water-limited=7 t/ha, actual=3.5 t/ha → Exploitable gap = 3.5 t/ha.
formulas.purpose_ar: يحلل الفجوة بين ما يحققه المزارع وما هو ممكن فيزيائياً.
formulas.variables_ar:
الإنتاجية المحتملة = محدودة بالماء والعناصر الغذائية، خالية من الآفات (محدودة بالإشعاع فقط)
الإنتاجية المحدودة بالماء = محدودة بالمياه، غير محدودة بالعناصر الغذائية، خالية من الآفات
الإنتاجية الفعلية = متوسط حقول المزارعين

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Yield gap analysis consistently shows that closing 50% of the exploitable gap globally would produce enough food for 10 billion people — without expanding agricultural land.

chapters.title: Mycorrhizal & Symbiotic Nitrogen Fixation Metrics
chapters.intro: Root symbioses with mycorrhizal fungi and rhizobia bacteria are among the most valuable biological services in agriculture. Quantifying these relationships justifies inoculant use and management decisions that preserve soil biology.

formulas.name: Root Colonization Index (RCI)
formulas.formula: RCI (%) = Colonized root segments / Total root segments examined × 100
formulas.variables:
Gridline intersect or magnified root staining method
Target: >50% for effective mycorrhizal benefit
Arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi (AMF): look for hyphae, vesicles, arbuscules
formulas.purpose: Proportion of root system colonized by mycorrhizal fungi; indicates potential P and water uptake benefit.
formulas.example: Examine 100 root segments: 64 show AMF structures → RCI = 64%.
formulas.pitfall: High P fertilization suppresses AMF colonization — monitor when reducing inputs.
formulas.purpose_ar: نظام الجذر المستعمَر بالفطريات الميكوريزية؛ يشير إلى الفائدة المحتملة في امتص الفوسفور والماء.
formulas.variables_ar:
طريقة تقاطع الشبكة أو صبغ الجذور المكبرة
الهدف: >50% للفائدة الفطرية الميكوريزية الفعالة
الفطريات الميكوريزية الوشيعية (AMF): ابحث عن الخيوط، الجراب، الجذور

formulas.name: Nitrogen Fixation Efficiency (NFE)
formulas.formula: NFE = Shoot N in inoculated / Shoot N in uninoculated (no N fertilizer control)
formulas.variables:
NFE > 1.5 indicates effective fixation
Measure at peak vegetative stage
Nodule count and colour also diagnostic (pink/red = active)
formulas.purpose: Compares N accumulation of inoculated vs uninoculated legumes without fertilizer N.
formulas.example: Inoculated soybean: 180 kg N/ha; control: 90 kg N/ha → NFE = 2.0 (excellent fixation).
formulas.purpose_ar: يقارن تراكم النيتروجين في البقوليات المُلقَّحة مقابل غير المُلقَّحة بدون سماد نيتروجيني.
formulas.variables_ar:
NFE > 1.5 يشير إلى تثبيت فعال
يُقاس في مرحلة النمو الخضري ذروة
عدد وتلون العقد الجذرية تشخيصيان أيضاً (وردي/أحمر = نشط)

formulas.name: Mycorrhizal Dependency (MD)
formulas.formula: MD (%) = (Yield with AMF − Yield without AMF) / Yield with AMF × 100
formulas.variables:
High MD = crop highly dependent on AMF for growth
Cassava MD ≈ 80–95%; maize ≈ 20–40%; barley ≈ 10–30%
formulas.purpose: Crop-level requirement for AMF; guides inoculant prioritization by crop species.
formulas.example: Cassava with AMF: 12 t/ha; without: 4 t/ha → MD = (12−4)/12×100 = 67%.
formulas.purpose_ar: متطلبات المستوى المحصولي للفطريات الميكوريزية؛ توجه أولويات اللقاح حسب نوع المحصول.
formulas.variables_ar:
MD مرتفع = المحصول يعتمد بشدة على الفطريات الميكوريزية للنمو
الكاسافا MD ≈ 80–95%؛ الذرة ≈ 20–40%؛ الشعير ≈ 10–30%

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Well-functioning mycorrhizal networks can reduce phosphorus fertilizer needs by 30–50% in highly dependent crops — an underutilized biological lever in soil fertility management.

chapters.title: Rumen Function & Fermentation Metrics
chapters.intro: Rumen fermentation is the engine of ruminant productivity. Indices of rumen health, volatile fatty acid profiles, and fiber digestion allow nutritionists to optimize rations for efficiency, health, and methane mitigation.

formulas.name: Acetate:Propionate Ratio (A:P)
formulas.formula: A:P = Molar proportion of acetate / Molar proportion of propionate
formulas.variables:
A:P = 3:1 typical for high-forage diet; 2:1 for high-concentrate
A:P < 2.2 = risk of sub-acute ruminal acidosis (SARA)
Acetate → fat; Propionate → glucose
formulas.purpose: Reflects fermentation pattern and energy substrate supply; shifts with diet composition.
formulas.example: Rumen fluid: acetate 65mol%, propionate 20mol%, butyrate 15mol% → A:P = 3.25.
formulas.pitfall: Sample via rumenocentesis or stomach tube — timing relative to feeding affects values.
formulas.purpose_ar: يعكس نمو التخمير وتوريد الطاقة؛ يتغير مع تكوين العلف.
formulas.variables_ar:
A:P = 3:1 نموذجي لنظام عالي الألياف؛ 2:1 لنظام عالي المركز
A:P < 2.2 = خطر الحموضة الحشوية الحادة (SARA)
الأسيتات → دهن؛ البروبيونات → جلوكوز

formulas.name: Effective NDF (eNDF)
formulas.formula: eNDF (% of diet DM) = NDF% × Physical effectiveness factor (pef)
formulas.variables:
pef = 1.0 for long forage; 0.5–0.8 for chopped; 0.2–0.4 for TMR grains
Minimum eNDF: 22% of diet DM for dairy cows
Below minimum → SARA risk, fat depression
formulas.purpose: NDF adjusted for particle size effect on rumen mat and chewing stimulation.
formulas.example: Silage NDF=40%, pef=0.8 → contributes 32% eNDF; grain NDF=10%, pef=0.2 → 2% eNDF.
formulas.purpose_ar: الألياف اللاذمة (NDF) معدلة لتأثير حجم الجسيم على كومة الحيوانات المجترة ومحفز المضغ.
formulas.variables_ar:
pef = 1.0 للأعلاف الطويلة؛ 0.5–0.8 للمفرومة؛ 0.2–0.4 للحبوب في نظام TMR
الحد الأدنى لـ eNDF: 22% من علف DM للأبقار الحليبة
أقل من الحد الأدنى → خطر SARA، انخفاض الدهن

formulas.name: Neutral Detergent Fiber Digestibility (NDFD)
formulas.formula: NDFD (%) = (NDF consumed − NDF in feces) / NDF consumed × 100
formulas.variables:
30-hour NDFD most used (in vitro): benchmark >55% for good forages
Higher NDFD → more energy from same NDF level
Drives DMI differences between forage sources
formulas.purpose: True digestibility of fiber fraction; major driver of dairy cow energy intake and yield.
formulas.example: Forage A: NDFD=62% (excellent); Forage B: NDFD=45% (poor) → choose A for dairy.
formulas.purpose_ar: هضم حقيقي لجزء الألياف؛ المحرك الرئيسي لاستهلاك الطاقة وعائد البقرة الحلوب.
formulas.variables_ar:
يُستخدم NDFD لمدة 30 ساعة (in vitro) بشكل أساسي: المعيار >55% للأعلاف الجيدة
ارتفاع NDFD → طاقة أكثر من نفس مستوى NDF
يحدد الفروق في استهلاك العلف بين مصادر الأعلاف المختلفة

formulas.name: Sub-Acute Ruminal Acidosis Risk Index (SARA-RI)
formulas.formula: SARA-RI = Hours per day rumen pH < 5.8
formulas.variables:
SARA diagnosed when pH < 5.8 for > 3 hours/day
Measured by continuous bolus pH loggers or spot rumenocentesis
Risk increases with high starch, low eNDF, rapid diet transition
formulas.purpose: Quantifies acidosis exposure time; defines SARA threshold for management intervention.
formulas.example: pH logger shows 4.5 hrs below 5.8 → SARA confirmed, increase buffer and forage particle size.
formulas.purpose_ar: يحدد وقت التعرض للحموضة؛ يعرّف عتبة SARA للتدبير الإداري.
formulas.variables_ar:
يُشخص SARA عندما يكون الرقم الهيدروجيني < 5.8 لمدة > 3 ساعات/يوم
يُقاس بواسطة مسجل pH مستمر أو بخزنة الحوم العشوائية
يزيد الخطر مع النشا العالي، eNDF المنخفض، الانتقال السريع في النظام الغذائي

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Optimizing rumen fermentation can reduce enteric methane production by 15–30% while maintaining or improving productivity — a key lever in livestock GHG mitigation.

chapters.title: Small Ruminant Production Metrics
chapters.intro: Sheep and goat production systems have distinct biological characteristics requiring adapted metrics. Applying bovine benchmarks to small ruminants leads to systematic errors in ration formulation, breeding decisions, and economic analysis.

formulas.name: Litter Size Index (LSI)
formulas.formula: LSI = Total lambs/kids born alive / Total ewes/does that lambed
formulas.variables:
Prolific breeds (Romanov, Booroola): LSI > 2.0
Merino: LSI 1.1–1.3; Dorper: 1.4–1.6
Does (goats) typically higher than ewes: 1.6–2.2
formulas.purpose: Average number of offspring per parturition; key breeding selection and nutritional management metric.
formulas.example: 100 ewes lamb, 148 lambs born alive → LSI = 1.48.
formulas.purpose_ar: متوسط عدد الأبناء لكل ولادة؛ مقياس رئيسي للاختيار التوليدي وإدارة التغذية.
formulas.variables_ar:
سلالات الخصوبة (رومانوف، بورولا): LSI > 2.0
ميرينو: LSI 1.1–1.3؛ دوربر: 1.4–1.6
الماعز (الماعز) عادة ما يكون أعلى من النعاج: 1.6–2.2

formulas.name: Fleece Weight & Fiber Quality Index
formulas.formula: Clean fleece weight (kg) = Greasy fleece × (100 − Vegetable matter% − Grease%) / 100
formulas.variables:
Fiber diameter in microns: superfine <17μm; fine 17–19; medium 23–26; strong >30μm
Comfort factor = % fibers < 30μm (target >95% for next-to-skin wear)
formulas.purpose: Clean wool yield and quality assessment for Merino and wool breed management.
formulas.example: Greasy fleece 5.5 kg, 18% grease, 5% VM → Clean fleece = 5.5×0.77 = 4.24 kg.
formulas.purpose_ar: تقييم العائد الجاف والجودة لصوف ميرينو وإدارة سلالات الصوف.
formulas.variables_ar:
قطر الألياف بالميكرون: فائق الدقة <17μm؛ دقيق 17–19؛ متوسط 23–26؛ قوي >30μm
عامل الراحة = % الألياف < 30μm (الهدف >95% للملابس التي تلامس الجلد)

formulas.name: Milk-to-Liveweight Ratio (MLR) — Dairy Goats
formulas.formula: MLR = Daily milk yield (kg) / Doe body weight (kg)
formulas.variables:
High-producing Saanen does: MLR 0.08–0.12
Alpine: 0.07–0.10
Used to compare breeds of different body size
formulas.purpose: Size-corrected milk production measure for dairy goat breed comparison.
formulas.example: Saanen doe: 4.5 kg/day, BW=65 kg → MLR = 0.069.
formulas.name_ar: نسبة الحليب إلى الوزن الحي (MLR) — الماعز الحلوب
formulas.purpose_ar: مقياس إنتاج الحليب المصحح حسب الحجم لمقارنة سلالات الماعز الحلوب.
formulas.variables_ar:
ماعز سانين المُنتِجة بكثافة: MLR 0.08–0.12
ألباين: 0.07–0.10
يُستخدم لمقارنة سلالات بأحجام جسم مختلفة

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Small ruminants are the primary livestock of smallholder and pastoral communities — their productivity metrics are essential for food security and income monitoring in the Global South.

chapters.title: Insect Farming Production Metrics
chapters.intro: Insect farming is among the fastest-growing food and feed sectors globally. Black soldier fly (Hermetia illucens), mealworm (Tenebrio molitor), and house cricket (Acheta domesticus) are the dominant commercial species, with distinct production metrics.

formulas.name: Substrate Conversion Efficiency (SCE)
formulas.formula: SCE (%) = Larval fresh weight gained / Substrate DM offered × 100
formulas.variables:
BSF: SCE 10–25% on organic waste substrates
Higher SCE on high-quality substrates (restaurant waste, brewery grains)
Different from FCR — substrate is often waste, not purpose-grown feed
formulas.purpose: Efficiency of converting organic substrate into insect biomass; key economic and environmental metric.
formulas.example: BSF larvae: 8 kg fresh weight from 50 kg substrate DM → SCE = 16%.
formulas.pitfall: SCE varies hugely with substrate quality — always report substrate composition.
formulas.purpose_ar: كفاءة تحويل الركيزة العضوية إلى كتلة الحشرات؛ مقياس اقتصادي وبيئي رئيسي.
formulas.variables_ar:
ذبابة الفول: SCE 10–25% على ركائز النفايات العضوية
كفاءة SCE أعلى على الركائع عالية الجودة (نفايات المطاعم، حبوب البيرة)
تختلف عن FCR — الركيزة غالبًا ما تكون نفايات، وليس غذاءً مُنَمَّىً

formulas.name: Insect Feed Conversion Ratio (iFCR)
formulas.formula: iFCR = Feed DM consumed / Insect DM gained  [kg/kg]
formulas.variables:
BSF on optimized diet: iFCR 1.4–2.0
Mealworm: iFCR 2.2–3.5
Cricket: iFCR 1.7–2.5
Compare on DM basis to avoid moisture content errors
formulas.purpose: Dry matter efficiency of feed conversion; allows cross-species comparison.
formulas.example: Mealworm: 100 kg feed DM, 35 kg insect DM gained → iFCR = 2.86.
formulas.purpose_ar: كفاءة تحويل المادة الجافة للغذاء؛ يسمح بالمقارنة عبر الأنواع.
formulas.variables_ar:
ذبابة الفول على نظام غذائي محسّن: iFCR 1.4–2.0
دودة العmealworm: iFCR 2.2–3.5
جراد الأرز: iFCR 1.7–2.5
المقارنة على أساس DM لتجنب أخطاء محتوى الرطوبة

formulas.name: Larval Yield Percentage (LY%)
formulas.formula: LY% = Harvest fresh weight / Total rearing unit biomass at peak × 100
formulas.variables:
Accounts for mortality during rearing
BSF target: >75% survival from egg to harvest
formulas.purpose: Overall biological efficiency of the rearing cycle from egg/neonate to harvest.
formulas.example: 500g eggs → expected maximum 20 kg larvae; achieved 16 kg → LY% = 80%.
formulas.purpose_ar: الكفاءة البيولوجية الإجمالية لدورة التربية من البيضة/اليرقة الطفيفة إلى الحصاد.
formulas.variables_ar:
يأخذ في الاعتبار الوفيات أثناء التربية
هدف ذبابة الفول: >75% بقاء من البيضة إلى الحصاد

formulas.name: Frass Fertilizer Value
formulas.variables:
BSF frass: ~2.5–4% N, 1.5–2.5% P₂O₅, 1.8–2.5% K₂O
Organic N — slower release than synthetic
Value as fertilizer partially offsets production cost
formulas.purpose: Economic and agronomic value of insect excrement and substrate residue as fertilizer.
formulas.example: BSF frass with 3% N → 300 kg N equivalent per tonne frass.
formulas.purpose_ar: القيمة الاقتصادية والزراعية لبراز الحشرات وفض الركيزة كسماد.
formulas.variables_ar:
فضلات ذبابة الفول: ~2.5–4% N، 1.5–2.5% P₂O₅، 1.8–2.5% K₂O
N عضوي — أبطأ إطلاق من الاصطناعي
القيمة كسماد تعوض جزئيًا عن تكلفة الإنتاج

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Insect protein production emits 50–100× less GHG per kg protein than beef, uses 10–100× less land, and can valorize organic wastes — circular economy potential is exceptional.

chapters.title: Equine Performance & Management Metrics
chapters.intro: Equine science bridges sport, work, and breeding applications. These metrics apply across racing, sport horse, and draught horse contexts, and are absent from most livestock handbooks.

formulas.name: Heart Rate Recovery Index (HRRI)
formulas.formula: HRRI = Resting HR / HR at 10 minutes post-exercise × 100
formulas.variables:
Fit horse recovers to <60 bpm within 10 min
HRRI > 80 = excellent fitness; < 60 = poor recovery
Used in endurance events as fitness qualification criterion
formulas.purpose: Speed of cardiovascular recovery after exercise; standard fitness assessment metric.
formulas.example: Resting HR=40 bpm, HR at 10 min=52 bpm → HRRI = 77 (good fitness).
formulas.pitfall: Dehydration and electrolyte imbalance impair recovery — assess alongside gut sounds.
formulas.purpose_ar: سرعة استرداد القلب والأوعية الدموية بعد التمرين؛ مقياس معياري لتقييم اللياقة.
formulas.variables_ar:
الحصان الليتع يستعيد إلى <60 نبضة/دقيقة خلال 10 دقائق
HRRI > 80 = لياقة ممتازة؛ < 60 = استرداد سيء
يُستخدم في أحداث التحمل كمعيار مؤهل لللياقة

formulas.name: Stride Length & Frequency
formulas.variables:
Elite thoroughbred: stride length 6.5–7.0 m, frequency 2.2–2.4 Hz at gallop
Stride length more trainable than frequency
Used in biomechanical gait analysis for lameness and optimization
formulas.purpose: Decomposes speed into its two biomechanical components for training and soundness assessment.
formulas.example: Horse at 16 m/s: stride length=6.8m → frequency = 16/6.8 = 2.35 strides/s.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب سرعة الحيوان من طول وتردد الخطوة.

formulas.name: Body Condition Score (BCS) — Equine
formulas.formula: Henneke scale: 1 (poor) to 9 (extremely fat); target 4–6 for performance; 5–7 for breeding mares
formulas.variables:
Assess at 6 sites: neck, withers, shoulder, ribs, loin, tailhead
BCS at covering predicts conception rate (target ≥5)
Score < 3 = welfare concern
formulas.purpose: Visual and palpation assessment of fat cover at standardized body sites.
formulas.example: Performance horse: maintain BCS 4–5; broodmare: BCS 5–6 at breeding.
formulas.purpose_ar: تقييم حالة الجسم للخيول على مقياس 1-9.

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Proper BCS management in broodmares can increase conception rates by 15–25% — one of the most cost-effective interventions in equine reproductive management.

chapters.title: Hyperspectral & Advanced Remote Sensing Indices
chapters.intro: Hyperspectral sensors capture 100–400 narrow spectral bands, enabling detection of specific plant biochemical compounds invisible to standard multispectral cameras. These indices are moving from research into commercial precision agriculture applications.

formulas.name: Red Edge Position (REP)
formulas.variables:
R700, R740 = reflectance at 700nm and 740nm
REP shifts 700→720nm as N status declines
Correlates strongly with leaf chlorophyll and N content
formulas.purpose: Position of maximum slope in the red-edge region; most sensitive indicator of plant nitrogen status from remote sensing.
formulas.example: REP = 717 nm (adequate N); REP = 708 nm (N stress — consider top-dressing).
formulas.pitfall: Requires hyperspectral sensor with <5nm band width — not available from Sentinel-2.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب موضع الحافة الحمراء في الطيف ل تقييم صحة النبات.

formulas.name: Anthocyanin Reflectance Index (ARI)
formulas.variables:
R550, R700 = reflectance at 550nm and 700nm
Higher ARI = more anthocyanin (stress, senescence, or variety trait)
Useful for detecting cold stress, P deficiency, and variety identification
formulas.purpose: Estimates leaf anthocyanin content; indicator of plant stress responses.
formulas.example: ARI > 0.2 on non-red-leaf varieties → investigate P deficiency or cold stress.
formulas.purpose_ar: قياس محتوى الأنثوسيانين في النباتات.

formulas.name: Carotenoid:Chlorophyll Index (CCI)
formulas.variables:
Rising CCI indicates senescence or stress (carotenoids persist as chlorophyll degrades)
Useful for harvest timing and canopy senescence mapping
formulas.purpose: Ratio indicating dominance of carotenoids over chlorophyll; rises during senescence.
formulas.example: Green crop: CCI ≈ 1.2; senescing crop: CCI ≈ 2.5+.
formulas.purpose_ar: نسبة الكاروتينويد إلى الكلوروفيل في النبات.

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Hyperspectral-guided variable-rate N application has demonstrated 20–35% fertilizer savings with no yield penalty in winter wheat and maize — the technology is approaching commercial viability.

chapters.title: Farm Management Software KPIs
chapters.intro: Modern farm management software (FMS) generates dashboards of KPIs. Understanding which metrics to prioritize, how they are calculated, and what actions they trigger is essential for translating digital tools into farm profit.

formulas.name: Cost of Production per Hectare (COP/ha)
formulas.formula: COP/ha = (Fixed costs + Variable costs) / Hectares farmed
formulas.variables: Fixed: depreciation, land rent, labour overhead
formulas.purpose: Foundation metric of farm enterprise economics; identifies break-even yield.
formulas.example: Fixed $400/ha + Variable $650/ha → COP = $1,050/ha. At $200/t price, break-even = 5.25 t/ha.
formulas.pitfall: Allocate shared costs (machinery, management) consistently across enterprises.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب إجمالي تكلفة الإنتاج لكل هكتار.

formulas.name: Input Use Efficiency Score (IUES)
formulas.formula: IUES = Yield achieved / Yield expected given inputs applied
formulas.variables:
Compares actual yield to model-predicted yield for the inputs used
IUES < 0.8 = inefficient input use — investigate agronomic losses
Built into some FMS platforms as "performance index"
formulas.purpose: Reveals whether inputs are converting efficiently into yield.
formulas.example: Expected yield 7 t/ha given inputs; achieved 5.5 t/ha → IUES = 0.79 (investigate).
formulas.purpose_ar: قياس كفاءة تحويل المدخلات إلى إنتاج.

formulas.name: Return on Data Investment (RODI)
formulas.formula: RODI = Value of decisions improved by data / Cost of data collection system
formulas.variables:
Quantify: input savings, yield improvement, reduced waste, insurance savings
Typical precision ag ROI: $25–75/ha/year above system cost
Most value from VRA fertilizer and targeted scouting
formulas.purpose: Economic justification for digital tools and precision agriculture investment.
formulas.example: Precision N saves $80/ha, system costs $30/ha → RODI = 2.67 ($2.67 return per $1 invested).
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب العائد المالي من استثمار أنظمة جمع البيانات.

chapters.title: Blockchain & Food Traceability Metrics
chapters.intro: Traceability systems — whether blockchain-based or conventional — must be evaluated on their completeness, speed, and reliability. These metrics help producers and buyers assess system performance and justify premium pricing.

formulas.name: Traceability Completeness Index (TCI)
formulas.variables:
Required attributes: origin, inputs, handling, testing, certification
TCI = 100% = fully traceable batch
Minimum for major retailers: >85%
formulas.purpose: Measures what proportion of required traceability data is actually recorded for a batch.
formulas.example: 14 of 16 required attributes recorded for a mango shipment → TCI = 87.5%.
formulas.pitfall: Identify highest-value missing attributes — origin and chemical residues typically most critical.
formulas.purpose_ar: نسبة السمات المتتبعة إلى السمات المطلوبة.

formulas.name: Time-to-Trace (TTT)
formulas.formula: TTT = Time from recall trigger to complete batch identification  [hours]
formulas.variables:
Industry target: <2 hours for fresh produce
FDA FSMA: < 24 hours for full trace-back
Blockchain systems achieve TTT < 2 seconds vs paper-based 6–7 days
formulas.purpose: Speed of tracing a contaminated batch through the supply chain; key food safety metric.
formulas.example: Walmart leafy greens blockchain: TTT reduced from 7 days to 2.2 seconds.
formulas.purpose_ar: الوقت اللازم لتتبع دفعة كاملة من المنتج.

formulas.name: Consumer Trust Premium (CTP)
formulas.formula: CTP = Price premium from certified traceable product / Certification cost
formulas.variables:
Organic: 20–100% premium; Rainforest Alliance: 5–15%; Fair Trade: 10–30%
Blockchain-verified: emerging premiums of 5–20%
Premiums highest in export and specialty markets
formulas.purpose: Economic return on traceability investment from market price premiums.
formulas.example: Cacao: $200/t premium for certification, $30/t certification cost → CTP = 6.7.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب العلاوة السعرية للمنتجات القابلة للتتبع.

chapters.title: Agricultural Robotics & Automation Metrics
chapters.intro: Autonomous tractors, harvesting robots, and seeding drones are entering commercial scale. Economic and performance metrics for these systems help producers make adoption and investment decisions.

formulas.name: Field Efficiency Index (FEI)
formulas.formula: FEI (%) = Effective field capacity / Theoretical field capacity × 100
formulas.variables:
Theoretical = speed × working width × 10⁻¹
Effective = actual ha/hr after turns, overlaps, downtime
Target: >75% for autonomous; conventional: 65–80%
formulas.purpose: Actual productivity relative to theoretical maximum; measures operational losses.
formulas.example: Autonomous tractor: theoretical 8 ha/hr, actual 6.4 ha/hr → FEI = 80%.
formulas.pitfall: Autonomous systems can improve FEI by reducing operator fatigue-related downtime.
formulas.purpose_ar: نسبة السعة الفعلية إلى السعة النظرية للمعدات.

formulas.name: Robotic Harvest Miss Rate & Damage Rate
formulas.formula:
Miss rate (%) = Unharvested fruit / Total fruit × 100
Damage rate (%) = Damaged fruit / Total harvested × 100
formulas.variables:
Commercial viability: miss rate <5%, damage rate <2%
Current sweet pepper robots: miss rate 10–25%, damage 3–8%
Technology rapidly improving — benchmark annually
formulas.purpose: Key quality and efficiency metrics for selective harvesting robots.
formulas.example: Apple robot: 50 fruit/tree, 3 missed, 2 damaged → miss=6%, damage=4% (near commercial).
formulas.name_ar: معدل الفشل والضرر في الحصاد الآلي
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب نسبة الفواكه غير المحصودة والمتضررة.

formulas.name: Automation ROI
formulas.formula: ROI (%) = (Labour savings + Yield improvement − System cost) / System cost × 100
formulas.variables:
Include: purchase, maintenance, training, connectivity costs
Typical autonomous tractor ROI: 15–35% over 5 years
Harvesting robots: ROI depends heavily on labour cost and availability
formulas.purpose: Financial return on automation investment; compares to alternative use of capital.
formulas.example: System cost $120k, annual labour saving $35k, 5-yr total saving $175k → ROI = 46%.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب العائد المالي من استثمارات الأتمتة الزراعية.

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Autonomous precision application robots can reduce herbicide use by 90%+ through spot-spraying only weeds — transforming the economics and environmental profile of weed management.

chapters.title: Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) in Agriculture
chapters.intro: Life Cycle Assessment quantifies environmental impacts of a product across its entire supply chain — from raw material inputs through production, processing, and disposal. Agricultural LCAs are increasingly required by food companies, lenders, and regulators.

formulas.name: Carbon Footprint (CF) — Farm Gate
formulas.formula: CF (kg CO₂-eq/kg product) = Σ(Activity × Emission factor) / Functional unit
formulas.variables:
Include: N₂O from fertilizer, CH₄ from livestock, CO₂ from energy, land use change
Functional unit: 1 kg milk, 1 kg grain, 1 kg live weight
Global warming potential (GWP100): N₂O=298, CH₄=25 (AR4)
formulas.purpose: Total greenhouse gas burden per unit of agricultural product; primary sustainability KPI.
formulas.example: Dairy: enteric CH₄ + manure N₂O + feed production CO₂ → typical 1.0–2.5 kg CO₂-eq/kg FPCM.
formulas.pitfall: Scope: include purchased feed and energy (Scope 3) for full picture.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب إجمالي انبعاثات الكربون لكل وحدة منتج.

formulas.name: Water Footprint (WF)
formulas.formula: WF = Blue WF + Green WF + Grey WF  [m³/tonne]
formulas.variables:
Blue = surface + groundwater consumed
Green = rainwater consumed (evapotranspiration)
Grey = water to dilute pollutant load to safe level
Beef: WF ≈ 15,400 m³/t; wheat: ≈ 1,830 m³/t
formulas.purpose: Total freshwater consumption and pollution footprint per unit of product.
formulas.example: Irrigated cotton: Blue WF=7,000m³/t, Green=5,000m³/t, Grey=200m³/t → WF=12,200m³/t.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب إجمالي استهلاك المياه لكل وحدة منتج.

formulas.name: Land Use Efficiency (LUE — LCA Context)
formulas.formula: LUE = 1 / (Land occupied m² × days / kg product)
formulas.variables:
Compares land productivity across systems and locations
Allows comparison of different production systems on a common basis
formulas.purpose: Land occupation efficiency; allows comparison of intensive vs extensive systems on product basis.
formulas.example: Tomato greenhouse: 0.5 m²·days/kg; field tomato: 2.3 m²·days/kg → 4.6× land efficiency.
formulas.purpose_ar: كفاءة استخدام الأراضي لإنتاج وحدة من المنتج.

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Agricultural LCA is evolving toward biodiversity impact, soil health, and animal welfare dimensions beyond carbon and water — expect more comprehensive reporting requirements by 2030.

chapters.title: Smallholder Profitability Toolkit
chapters.intro: Standard economic analysis methods assume reliable data, market access, and monetized production. This chapter adapts core economic metrics for smallholder and subsistence farming contexts where data is sparse and food security is paramount.

formulas.name: Partial Budget Analysis (PBA)
formulas.formula: Net change = (Added returns + Reduced costs) − (Added costs + Reduced returns)
formulas.variables:
Used to evaluate a single management change without full farm budget
Positive net change = beneficial change; negative = avoid
Only account for items that change — ignore fixed costs
formulas.purpose: Rapid economic screening for single technology or practice changes.
formulas.example: New variety: +$200 yield revenue, −$30 seed cost, −$20 extra labour → Net change = +$150/ha.
formulas.pitfall: Most powerful when combined with yield data from local on-farm trials.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب التغير الصافي في العائد والتكاليف من تغيير معين.

formulas.name: Return to Family Labour (RFL)
formulas.formula: RFL ($/day) = Net farm income / Family labour days
formulas.variables:
Compare to local casual labour wage
RFL < wage rate = farm labour not competitive — investigate
Include all unpaid household labour (commonly undervalued by 40–60%)
formulas.purpose: Value of family labour contribution; critical where cash income and food production overlap.
formulas.example: Net income $1,200, 120 family labour days → RFL = $10/day (compare to $8/day local wage = viable).
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب صافي دخل المزرعة لكل يوم عمل عائلي.

formulas.name: Break-even Yield (BEY)
formulas.formula: BEY = Total variable costs / Crop price  [t/ha or units/ha]
formulas.variables:
Compare BEY to expected yield and historical yields
If expected yield < BEY → do not invest in that input package
Risk buffer: add 20% to BEY for risk management
formulas.purpose: Minimum yield required to cover variable costs; critical input decision threshold for smallholders.
formulas.example: Variable costs $400/ha, price $160/t → BEY = 2.5 t/ha. Expected yield 3.5 t/ha → profitable.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب الحد الأدنى للإنتاج لتغطية التكاليف المتغيرة.

formulas.name: Subsistence Value of Home Consumption
formulas.formula: Subsistence value = Quantity consumed × Local market retail price
formulas.variables:
Often 30–70% of smallholder farm output
Must include in total farm revenue calculations
Critical for food security and welfare analysis
formulas.purpose: Monetary value of food produced and consumed at home; frequently excluded from farm income calculations.
formulas.example: Family consumes 500 kg grain at $0.35/kg market price → Subsistence value = $175/yr.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب قيمة المنتجات المستهلكة محلياً.

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Including subsistence food value in income calculations typically increases measured smallholder welfare by 30–50% — critical for non-distorted policy design.

chapters.title: Land Rent & Tenure Economics
chapters.intro: Land access and tenure security are fundamental determinants of agricultural investment and productivity. These metrics are essential for policy analysis, land valuation, and farm business planning involving leased or shared land.

formulas.name: Land Rent Capitalization Rate
formulas.formula: Land value = Annual net rent / Capitalization rate
formulas.variables:
Capitalization rate = risk-free rate + land risk premium (typically 3–6%)
Net rent = gross rent − property taxes, maintenance
Annual rent = Land value × Cap rate
formulas.purpose: Links annual land rental value to capital value; used for land purchase decisions.
formulas.example: Annual net rent $400/ha, cap rate 4% → Land value = $10,000/ha.
formulas.pitfall: Cap rates vary by region, crop potential, and irrigation access — use local comparables.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب قيمة الأرض من الإيجار السنوي ومعدل الرسملة.

formulas.name: Sharecropping Efficiency Index (SEI)
formulas.formula: SEI = (Actual output under share tenancy / Potential output under owner-operation)
formulas.variables:
SEI < 1 due to Marshallian disincentive effect (tenant gets only share of marginal gain)
Theory predicts SEI = share ratio (e.g., 0.5 for 50:50 crop sharing)
Monitoring SEI guides contract restructuring
formulas.purpose: Efficiency loss attributable to sharecropping tenure relative to owner-operated baseline.
formulas.example: Owner-operated plots yield 5 t/ha; tenant-operated (50% share) yield 4.1 t/ha → SEI = 0.82.
formulas.purpose_ar: مقارنة الإنتاج الفعلي بالمحتمل تحت نظام المشاركة.

formulas.name: Leasehold vs Ownership NPV Comparison
formulas.formula:
NPV_lease = Σ[Farm profit − Rent] / (1+r)^t
NPV_own = Σ[Farm profit] / (1+r)^t − Land price
formulas.variables:
Compare NPVs over 10–20 year horizon
If NPV_own > NPV_lease → buying is preferable (at that land price)
Sensitivity to land price appreciation significantly affects result
formulas.purpose: Guides the buy-vs-lease decision for land expansion using discounted cash flow analysis.
formulas.example: At 4% discount rate and 2% land appreciation: ownership becomes superior beyond ~15 years.
formulas.name_ar: مقارنة القيمة الحالية الصافية: الإيجار مقابل الملكية
formulas.purpose_ar: مقارنة القيمة الحالية الصافية للإيجار والملكية.

rows: Composite of title, lease length, dispute rate

chapters.title: Circular Economy on the Farm
chapters.intro: Circular agriculture minimizes waste by converting farm by-products into inputs for other system components. These metrics quantify resource cycling efficiency and guide investment in circular infrastructure.

formulas.name: Nutrient Recycling Efficiency (NRE)
formulas.formula: NRE (%) = Nutrients returned to soil / Nutrients harvested in products × 100
formulas.variables:
Include manure, crop residues, compost, recycled irrigation water
NRE = 100% = all harvested nutrients returned (closed loop)
Typical conventional farms: NRE 20–40%; integrated crop-livestock: 50–80%
formulas.purpose: Proportion of extracted nutrients that are recycled within the farm system.
formulas.example: Harvested N: 150 kg/ha; returned via manure and residues: 90 kg/ha → NRE = 60%.
formulas.pitfall: High NRE reduces fertilizer purchase and environmental nutrient losses simultaneously.
formulas.purpose_ar: نسبة العناصر الغذائية المعادة إلى التربة إلى المحصودة.

formulas.name: Manure Nitrogen Recovery (MNR)
formulas.formula: MNR (%) = Crop N uptake from manure / Total N in manure applied × 100
formulas.variables:
First year recovery: 30–60% for solid manure; 60–80% for slurry
Remaining N mineralizes in subsequent years
Injection > surface application for ammonia conservation
formulas.purpose: Efficiency of converting manure N into crop N uptake; guides manure management.
formulas.example: 15 t/ha cattle slurry at 2.5% N = 375 kg N; crop takes up 195 kg N → MNR = 52%.
formulas.purpose_ar: نسبة النيتروجين الممتص من السماد العضوي.

formulas.name: Energy Self-Sufficiency Ratio (ESR)
formulas.formula: ESR = On-farm energy production / Total farm energy consumption
formulas.variables:
ESR > 1 = net energy exporter
Biogas, solar PV, biomass boilers contribute to ESR
Typical integrated biogas farm: ESR 0.6–1.2
formulas.purpose: Proportion of energy needs met by on-farm renewable production.
formulas.example: Farm uses 150 MWh/yr, biogas system produces 110 MWh → ESR = 0.73 (73% energy self-sufficient).
formulas.purpose_ar: نسبة إنتاج الطاقة في المزرعة إلى استهلاكها.

formulas.name: Crop Residue Utilization Rate (CRUR)
formulas.formula: CRUR (%) = Residue used productively / Total residue produced × 100
formulas.variables:
Productive uses: livestock feed, biogas, compost, mulch, bioenergy
Burning = 0% CRUR (waste + pollution)
Target: CRUR > 70% in integrated systems
formulas.purpose: Proportion of crop residues converted to productive uses rather than burned or wasted.
formulas.example: 8 t/ha maize stover: 5 t fed to cattle, 2 t composted, 1 t left on field → CRUR = 87.5%.
formulas.purpose_ar: نسبة بقايا المحاصيل المستخدمة بشكل منتج.

rows: Crop N from manure / Manure N × 100
Depends on manure type and application

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Farms with high circularity indices (>0.7) typically reduce input costs by 20–35% while achieving equal or better yields — the circular model is both profitable and resilient.

parts.title: Visual Guides, Decision Tools & Global Content

chapters.title: Management Decision Flowcharts
chapters.intro:
Decision trees translate index values into clear management actions. These visual guides are the highest-value extension tool — bridging the gap between "what does this number mean" and "what should I do about it."
Quick Reference

rows: Soil Olsen P or leaf P%
Check irrigation water EC and LR
If not salt, check seed quality + pest
Audit ration energy density and DMI
Review BCS, heat detection, semen quality
Light hours, feed, age, disease assessment
Deep rip + cover crop with tap root
Add compost, reduce tillage, cover crops
Apply gypsum + leach + plant tolerant crop
Reduce inputs, add compost, diverse rotation

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Decision trees that link index values to specific actions are consistently rated by farmers and extension agents as the most practically useful content in any agricultural guide.

chapters.title: Multilingual Glossary of Key Terms
chapters.intro:
This glossary provides translations of the most important agricultural metrics and abbreviations in Arabic, French, Spanish, and Swahili — the four languages covering the largest agricultural readership outside English.
Quick Reference

rows: Eficiencia en el uso del agua

chapters.title: Tropical & Subtropical Crop Metrics
chapters.intro: The handbook was originally weighted toward temperate crops. This chapter extends coverage to major tropical staples and cash crops of Sub-Saharan Africa, South and Southeast Asia, and Latin America.

formulas.name: Cassava Harvest Index & Starch Index
formulas.formula:
HI = Fresh root weight / Total fresh plant weight × 100
Starch index (SI) = Root dry matter% × HI / 100
formulas.variables:
Cassava HI target: 55–70% fresh weight basis
Root DM% of 30–40% indicates high starch
SI > 20 = high-value processing cassava
formulas.purpose: Cassava-specific adaptation of harvest index and starch quality metric for industrial processors.
formulas.example: Fresh roots 15 kg, total plant 22 kg → HI = 68%. Root DM 35% → SI = 0.35×68 = 23.8.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب مؤشر الحصاد ومؤشر النشا لجذور الكسافا.
formulas.part: Visual Guides, Decision Tools & Global Content

formulas.name: Cacao Pod Value Index (PVI)
formulas.formula: PVI = Bean weight per pod / Pod fresh weight × 100  (target: >10%)
formulas.variables:
Pod index = number of pods to produce 1 kg dry beans (target: <18)
Fermentation quality: 3–7 days determines final cup quality
Dry bean recovery: fresh fermented beans / dry beans ≈ 2.2:1
formulas.purpose: Productivity and quality metrics for cacao — the difference between fine and bulk chocolate.
formulas.example: 18 pods, each 350g, each 40g dry beans → Pod index = 18 pods/0.72kg = 25 (poor — investigate variety).
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب نسبة وزن البذور إلى وزن القرن للكاكاو.
formulas.part: Visual Guides, Decision Tools & Global Content

formulas.name: Banana Bunch Weight Index (BWI)
formulas.formula: BWI = Bunch weight (kg) / Number of hands × Number of fingers
formulas.variables:
Target: finger weight >200g for export grade (Cavendish)
Bunch weight 25–45 kg is commercial target
Days from flowering to harvest: 90–120 days depending on altitude
formulas.purpose: Measures bunch filling efficiency; guides fertilization and crop load management.
formulas.example: 30 kg bunch, 9 hands, 18 fingers per hand → BWI = 30/(9×18) = 185g/finger (borderline export).
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب متوسط وزن الإصبع من وزن الخردلة.
formulas.part: Visual Guides, Decision Tools & Global Content

formulas.name: Coffee Cup Score & Screen Size
formulas.formula:
SCA cup score: 80–100 (specialty); <80 (commercial)
Screen size: % retained on designated screen (e.g., Screen 18 = 7.14mm)
formulas.variables:
Screen 18+ = premium grade; Screen 15 = commercial grade
Q Grader certified cupping uses SCA 100-point protocol
Cup score: aroma, flavor, aftertaste, acidity, body, uniformity, balance, sweetness
formulas.purpose: International quality grading system for green coffee; determines market price category.
formulas.example: Screen 18: 85%, Screen 17: 10%, below: 5% → Premium grade (>80% screen 18 required for specialty).
formulas.purpose_ar: تقييم جودة القهوة بناءً على درجة التذوق وحجم الحبوب.
formulas.part: Visual Guides, Decision Tools & Global Content

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Tropical crops support the livelihoods of over 500 million smallholder farmers — standardized metrics for these crops are urgently needed for market access and productivity improvement programs.

chapters.title: Dryland & Rainfed Farming Metrics
chapters.intro: Over 80% of global cropland is rainfed. Metrics adapted for water-limited, low-input systems are essential for the majority of the world's farmers, yet are underrepresented in most agronomy references.

formulas.name: Rainfall Use Efficiency (RUE)
formulas.purpose: Primary productivity metric for rainfed systems; tracks efficiency of converting rain to yield.
formulas.example: Sorghum: 2,800 kg/ha with 380 mm rain → RUE = 7.4 kg/ha/mm.
formulas.pitfall: RUE is sensitive to distribution, not just total rainfall — a drought at flowering depresses RUE severely.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب الإنتاج لكل ملليمتر من أمطار موسم النمو.
formulas.part: Visual Guides, Decision Tools & Global Content

formulas.name: Water Harvest Index (WHI)
formulas.formula: WHI = Grain yield (kg/ha) / Total water use (rainfall + stored soil water, mm)
formulas.variables: More comprehensive than RUE — includes stored water drawdown
formulas.purpose: Efficiency of converting all available water (rain + soil store) into grain.
formulas.example: Wheat: 2,400 kg/ha, 280 mm rain + 60 mm soil water used → WHI = 2400/340 = 7.1 kg/ha/mm.
formulas.purpose_ar: كفاءة تحويل إجمالي المياه المتاحة إلى إنتاج حبي.
formulas.part: Visual Guides, Decision Tools & Global Content

formulas.name: Drought Tolerance Index (DTI)
formulas.formula: DTI = (Ys × Yd) / (Yp)²  where Ys = stressed yield, Yd = drought yield, Yp = potential yield
formulas.variables:
Fischer-Maurer index: higher DTI = better drought tolerance without yield penalty under good conditions
Used in variety selection and breeding program screening
formulas.purpose: Combines yield stability under drought with absolute productivity; identifies varieties good in both environments.
formulas.example: Variety A: Ys=3.5, Yd=2.1, Yp=4.0 → DTI = (3.5×2.1)/16 = 0.46. Variety B DTI=0.38 → choose A.
formulas.purpose_ar: تقييم قدرة الأصناف على تحمل الجفاف مع الحفاظ على الإنتاج.
formulas.part: Visual Guides, Decision Tools & Global Content

formulas.name: Runoff Coefficient (RC)
formulas.variables:
Bare soil, slope >5%: RC = 0.4–0.7
Good ground cover, flat: RC = 0.1–0.2
Reduce RC to increase infiltration and stored soil water
formulas.purpose: Proportion of rainfall that runs off rather than infiltrating; key for water conservation planning.
formulas.example: 50 mm rain, 18 mm measured runoff → RC = 0.36. Conservation target: RC < 0.15 with good cover.
formulas.purpose_ar: نسبة مياه الجريان السطحي إلى إجمالي هطول الأمطار.
formulas.part: Visual Guides, Decision Tools & Global Content

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: In dryland Africa, a 20% improvement in RUE through better varieties and mulching would increase food production by an estimated 30–40 million tonnes per year.

chapters.title: Food Systems & Nutrition Metrics
chapters.intro: Agricultural productivity metrics must ultimately connect to human nutritional outcomes. Food systems metrics bridge the gap between farm yield and dietary quality, increasingly required by development funders, governments, and food companies.

formulas.name: Nutrient Density per Hectare (NutriDens)
formulas.formula: NutriDens_nutrient = Yield (kg/ha) × Nutrient content (per kg) / Daily requirement
formulas.variables:
Calculate separately for protein, energy, vitamin A, iron, zinc
Compare across crops to identify nutritionally efficient land uses
Legumes dominate on protein density; orange-fleshed crops on vitamin A
formulas.purpose: Quantifies how much of a daily nutritional requirement one hectare can supply; guides crop mix for nutrition security.
formulas.example: Cowpea: 1.2 t/ha × 215g protein/kg / 50g daily requirement = 5,160 person-days protein/ha.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب عدد أيام الشخص التي يمكن أن يوفرها الهكتار.
formulas.part: Visual Guides, Decision Tools & Global Content

formulas.name: Diet Quality Score from Farm Production
formulas.formula: DQS = Σ(food groups produced on farm or purchased / Required food groups) × 100
formulas.variables:
Based on FAO/WHO recommended 10 food groups
DQS = 100% = all food groups supplied from farm + income
Track monthly — seasonal gaps are common stress points
formulas.purpose: Tracks dietary diversity achievable from the farm system and income it generates.
formulas.example: Farm produces 6 of 10 food groups adequately → DQS = 60% (needs supplementation of dairy/animal foods).
formulas.name_ar: درجة جودة النظام الغذائي من إنتاج المزرعة
formulas.purpose_ar: تقييم تنوع وتغطية المجموعات الغذائية من المزرعة.
formulas.part: Visual Guides, Decision Tools & Global Content

formulas.name: Cost of Nutritious Diet (CoND)
formulas.formula: CoND ($/person/day) = Minimum cost of a diet meeting all nutrient requirements from local foods
formulas.variables:
Compare to household income per person per day
CoND > 30% of income = diet affordability problem
Used to set wage floors and food price policy targets
formulas.purpose: Minimum daily expenditure for a nutritionally adequate diet using locally available foods.
formulas.example: CoND in Sub-Saharan Africa: $1.50–3.50/person/day vs World Bank extreme poverty line $2.15.
formulas.purpose_ar: الحد الأدنى لتكلفة نظام غذائي يلبي جميع الاحتياجات الغذائية.
formulas.part: Visual Guides, Decision Tools & Global Content

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: A diet rich in diverse farm produce from smallholder farms delivers better nutritional outcomes than monoculture commodity supply chains — measuring nutrition per hectare makes this case quantitatively.

chapters.title: Global Benchmark Reference Database
chapters.intro:
This chapter provides typical ranges, global benchmarks, and regional norms for the key metrics in this handbook. Use these ranges to check plausibility of your calculations and identify performance gaps.
Quick Reference

chapters.title: Seasonal Farm Management Calendars
chapters.intro:
Linking metric monitoring to the seasonal farm calendar transforms a reference handbook into an operational management guide. This chapter shows when each key metric should be measured, monitored, and acted upon across major farming systems.
Quick Reference

rows: Grain moisture, NDVI trend, HI estimate
Grain MC%, HI, yield monitor data
BCS change, DMI, milk fever incidence
Transition diet review if >8% milk fever
ECM, BCS loss rate, conception rate
CI, calving rate, SCC annual mean, CIM
NRE: manure N available for crop
WUE, THI — share irrigation if needed
CRUR: proportion of residue allocated to livestock
BCS, ADG, carrying capacity on dry pasture

sustainability_notes: ♻  Sustainability Note: Integrated crop-livestock systems managed with aligned seasonal monitoring calendars consistently outperform separate systems in land productivity, resilience, and soil health over 5–10 year horizons.

chapters.title: Hydraulics Fundamentals
chapters.intro: Irrigation engineering equations for hydraulics fundamentals — sourced from the Irrigation Atlas (irrigationatlas.com).

formulas.name: Continuity Equation
formulas.variables:
Q = Flow Rate (m³/s)
A = Area (m²)
v = Velocity (m/s)
formulas.purpose: Calculates flow rate based on area and velocity.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب معدل التصرف المائي بناءً على مساحة المقطع والسرعة.
formulas.variables_ar:
Q = التصرف (م³/ثانية)
A = المساحة (م²)
v = السرعة (م/ث)

formulas.name: Bernoulli Equation
formulas.variables:
p = Pressure (Pa)
gamma = Spec. Weight (9810 N/m³)
v = Velocity (m/s)
g = Gravity (9.81 m/s²)
z = Elevation (m)
formulas.purpose: Energy conservation for fluids; relates pressure, velocity, elevation.
formulas.purpose_ar: قانون حفظ الطاقة للسوائل؛ يربط الضغط والسرعة والارتفاع.
formulas.variables_ar:
p = الضغط (با)
gamma = الوزن النوعي (9810 نيوتن/م³)
v = السرعة (م/ث)
g = الجاذبية (9.81 م/ث²)
z = المنسوب (م)

formulas.name: Reynolds Number
formulas.variables:
Re = Reynolds Number (-)
rho = Density (1000 kg/m³)
v = Velocity (m/s)
D = Diameter (m)
formulas.purpose: Determines flow regime (Laminar or Turbulent).
formulas.purpose_ar: يستخدم لتحديد حالة الجريان (صفائحي أو مضطرب).
formulas.variables_ar:
Re = رقم رينولدز (بدون وحدة)
rho = كثافة الماء (1000 كجم/م³)
v = السرعة (م/ث)
D = القطر (م)

formulas.name: Froude Number
formulas.variables:
Fr = Froude Number
v = Velocity (m/s)
g = Gravity (9.81)
y = Depth (m)

parts.title: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)
parts.subtitle: Peer-reviewed formulas from international standards bodies

chapters.title: FAO-56 Penman-Monteith Family
chapters.intro: Reference evapotranspiration, dual Kc, stress coefficients from FAO Irrigation & Drainage Paper 56 (Allen et al., 1998).

formulas.name: Penman-Monteith ETo (FAO-56)
formulas.variables:
Δ = slope of saturation vapor pressure curve (kPa/°C)
Rn = net radiation (MJ/m²/day)
G = soil heat flux (MJ/m²/day)
γ = psychrometric constant (kPa/°C)
T = mean air temp (°C)
u₂ = wind speed at 2m (m/s)
es = saturation VP (kPa)
ea = actual VP (kPa)
formulas.purpose: Reference evapotranspiration — the global standard for irrigation scheduling.
formulas.pitfall: Using wind speed at wrong height — must be 2m, or adjust with FAO-56 Eq. 47.
formulas.decision: Use this for all FAO-56 Kc × ETo irrigation calculations.
formulas.notes: Source: FAO Irrigation & Drainage Paper 56 (Allen et al., 1998), Eq. 6.
formulas.purpose_ar: التبخر-نتح المرجعي — المعيار العالمي لجدولة الري.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Dual Crop Coefficient (Kc = Kcb + Ke)
formulas.variables:
Kcb = basal crop coefficient
Ks = water stress coefficient (0-1)
Ke = soil evaporation coefficient
formulas.purpose: Separates plant transpiration from soil evaporation for precision irrigation.
formulas.pitfall: Forgetting Ks during deficit irrigation; over-estimating Ke after rain.
formulas.decision: Use dual Kc for daily irrigation scheduling; single Kc for weekly planning.
formulas.notes: Source: FAO-56 Chapter 7, Eq. 71. Ks = (TAW - Dr) / ((1-p) × TAW).
formulas.purpose_ar: يفصل نتح النبات عن تبخر التربة للري الدقيق.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Water Stress Coefficient (Ks)
formulas.variables:
TAW = total available water (mm)
Dr = root zone depletion (mm)
p = depletion fraction (0.3-0.7)
formulas.purpose: Quantifies reduction in ET under water stress — enables deficit irrigation planning.
formulas.pitfall: Using p for one crop on another — p varies by crop and ETc rate.
formulas.decision: Trigger irrigation when Ks < 0.85 for most crops; <0.70 for stress-tolerant.
formulas.notes: Source: FAO-56 Eq. 84. Default p=0.5 for most crops; see Table 22.
formulas.purpose_ar: يحدد انخفاض النتح تحت الإجهاد المائي لتمكين التخطيط للري الناقص.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Salinity-Adjusted ET (FAO-56)
formulas.variables:
ECe = electrical conductivity of saturation extract (dS/m)
b = crop-specific slope (% yield decrease per dS/m)
Kc = crop coefficient
ETo = reference ET
formulas.purpose: Reduces ET estimate when soil salinity limits water uptake.
formulas.example: Tomato (b=10%), ECe=4 dS/m, Kc=1.15, ETo=5 → ETc_adj = 1.15×5×(1-4×10/100) = 4.83 mm/day (vs 5.75 unstressed).
formulas.pitfall: Using threshold ECt instead of actual ECe; forgetting b varies by crop.
formulas.decision: If ETc_adj < 80% of ETc, leach salts before planting.
formulas.notes: Source: FAO-56 Chapter 8, Eq. 98. See Table 14 for crop-specific b values.
formulas.purpose_ar: يخفض تقدير النتح عندما تحد الملوحة من امتصاص الماء.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Net Radiation (Rn) — FAO-56
formulas.variables:
Rns = net shortwave radiation (MJ/m²/day) = (1-α)×Rs
Rnl = net longwave radiation (MJ/m²/day)
α = albedo (0.23 for crops)
Rs = solar radiation
formulas.purpose: Required input for Penman-Monteith; converts raw solar radiation to net energy.
formulas.pitfall: Using α=0.05 (water) for crops; forgetting Rnl depends on cloud cover.
formulas.notes: Source: FAO-56 Eq. 38-40. Rnl depends on T, ea, Rs/Rso ratio.
formulas.purpose_ar: مدخل مطلوب لمعادلة بينمان-مونتيث.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Psychrometric Constant (γ)
formulas.variables:
P = atmospheric pressure (kPa)
γ = psychrometric constant (kPa/°C)
formulas.purpose: Required for Penman-Monteith; varies with altitude.
formulas.pitfall: Using sea-level γ at high altitude — introduces 10-15% error in ETo.
formulas.purpose_ar: مطلوب لمعادلة بينمان-مونتيث ويختلف مع الارتفاع.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Saturation Vapor Pressure (es) — FAO-56
formulas.variables:
T = air temperature (°C)
es = saturation vapor pressure (kPa)
formulas.purpose: Core thermodynamic function for all ET and VPD calculations.
formulas.pitfall: Using Tetens with Celsius vs Fahrenheit; mixing kPa and mb.
formulas.decision: Use this exact form for FAO-56; Murray (1967) is equivalent.
formulas.purpose_ar: الدالة الديناميكية الحرارية الأساسية لكل حسابات النتح.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

chapters.title: USDA-NRCS Irrigation Engineering
chapters.intro: From National Engineering Handbook Part 652 — Christiansen uniformity, Manning's channel flow, furrow design, infiltration.

formulas.name: Christiansen Uniformity Coefficient (CU)
formulas.variables:
xᵢ = individual catch value
x̄ = mean of all catches
n = number of catch cans
formulas.purpose: Measures sprinkler irrigation uniformity — target CU > 84% for most crops.
formulas.example: 16 catches averaging 10mm; sum of deviations = 12mm → CU = 100×(1-12/160) = 92.5%.
formulas.pitfall: Using only edge catches; ignoring wind drift.
formulas.decision: CU < 75%: redesign sprinkler spacing or pressure.
formulas.notes: Source: Christiansen (1942); USDA-NRCS NEH Part 652, Ch. 11.
formulas.purpose_ar: يقيس تجانس الري بالرش.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Manning's Flow in Irrigation Channels
formulas.variables:
Q = flow rate (m³/s)
n = Manning's roughness (0.013 concrete, 0.025 earth)
A = cross-sectional area (m²)
R = hydraulic radius (m) = A/P
S = channel slope (m/m)
formulas.purpose: Design open-channel irrigation systems — furrows, canals, laterals.
formulas.pitfall: Using concrete n on vegetated channels (can underestimate by 3×).
formulas.decision: Keep velocity < 0.6 m/s in earth channels to prevent erosion.
formulas.purpose_ar: تصميم قنوات الري المفتوحة.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Furrow Inflow Design (USDA-NRCS)
formulas.variables:
Q_in = furrow inflow (L/s)
L = furrow length (m)
q_req = required infiltration depth (mm)
F = furrow spacing (m)
T = application time (hours)
formulas.purpose: Size the inflow rate at the head of a furrow for uniform infiltration.
formulas.example: L=200m, q_req=50mm, F=1m, T=4h → Q_in = (200×50×1)/(3.6×4) = 694 L/s — too high, shorten furrow.
formulas.pitfall: Ignoring furrow advance time; underestimating T.
formulas.decision: If Q_in > 3 L/s, split furrow into sets.
formulas.purpose_ar: تحديد معدل التدفق لكل أخدود للري المنتظم.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Available Water Capacity (AWC) by Texture
formulas.variables:
FC = field capacity (% by weight)
PWP = permanent wilting point (%)
D = root depth (cm)
AWC = available water (mm)
formulas.purpose: Estimate plant-available water for irrigation scheduling.
formulas.pitfall: Using % by weight without bulk density → off by 20-50%.
formulas.decision: Irrigate at 50% depletion (p=0.5) for most crops.
formulas.notes: Source: USDA Soil Survey Manual Handbook 18; NRCS Part 618.
formulas.purpose_ar: تقدير الماء المتاح للنبات لجدولة الري.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Horton Infiltration Model
formulas.variables:
f(t) = infiltration rate at time t (mm/h)
fc = final (asymptotic) infiltration rate
f₀ = initial infiltration rate
k = decay constant (1/h)
t = time since start (h)
formulas.purpose: Predict how fast water enters soil during irrigation or rainfall.
formulas.pitfall: Using lab f₀ on crusted field soil (real f₀ may be 50% lower).
formulas.decision: If f(t) < application rate, reduce flow or increase set time.
formulas.purpose_ar: يتنبأ بسرعة دخول الماء للتربة أثناء الري.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Sprinkler Application Rate
formulas.variables:
I = application rate (mm/h)
Q = sprinkler discharge (L/min)
S₁ = spacing along lateral (m)
S₂ = spacing between laterals (m)
formulas.purpose: Match sprinkler rate to soil infiltration to prevent runoff.
formulas.pitfall: I > f(t) causes runoff; common with clay soils and impact sprinklers.
formulas.decision: If I > infiltration rate, use lower-flow nozzles or wider spacing.
formulas.purpose_ar: مطابقة معدل الرش مع تسرب التربة لمنع الجريان.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

chapters.title: ASABE Drip & Sprinkler Standards
chapters.intro: From ASABE EP405.1 (drip) and EP458 (sprinkler) — emitter flow, emission uniformity, lateral hydraulics.

formulas.name: Drip Emitter Flow Variation (qv)
formulas.variables:
qmax = maximum emitter flow
qmin = minimum emitter flow
qavg = average flow
qv = flow variation (%)
formulas.purpose: Evaluate uniformity of drip irrigation systems.
formulas.pitfall: Measuring only 2-3 emitters; need ≥ 18 for statistical validity.
formulas.decision: qv < 10% excellent; 10-20% acceptable; >20% redesign.
formulas.notes: Source: ASABE EP405.1; also see EU (emission uniformity).
formulas.purpose_ar: تقييم تجانس أنظمة الري بالتنقيط.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Emission Uniformity (EU) — ASABE
formulas.variables:
CV = coefficient of variation of emitters
qmin = minimum emitter flow (lowest 25%)
qavg = average flow
formulas.purpose: Comprehensive drip uniformity metric — accounts for manufacturing variation + pressure differences.
formulas.pitfall: Confusing qv (simpler) with EU — EU is the ASABE standard.
formulas.decision: EU > 90% excellent; 80-90% acceptable; <80% redesign.
formulas.purpose_ar: مقياس شامل لتجانس الري بالتنقيط.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Pressure-Compensating Emitter Range
formulas.variables:
q = emitter flow (L/h)
k = emitter constant
P = pressure (kPa)
x = exponent (0 for PC, 0.5 for turbulent, 1 for laminar)
formulas.purpose: Predict emitter flow at different pressures — key for slope design.
formulas.pitfall: Using x=0.5 for PC emitters (they have x≈0, no pressure sensitivity).
formulas.decision: Use PC emitters (x≈0) on slopes >5% or laterals >100m.
formulas.notes: Source: ASABE EP458; also Keller & Bliesner (1990).
formulas.purpose_ar: يتنبأ بتدفق النقاط عند ضغوط مختلفة.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Lateral Head Loss (Darcy-Weisbach simplified)
formulas.variables:
hf = head loss (m)
Q = flow (L/s)
C = Hazen-Williams coefficient (150 plastic)
L = length (m)
D = inside diameter (mm)
formulas.purpose: Size drip/sprinkler laterals to keep pressure variation < 20%.
formulas.pitfall: Forgetting multiple-outlet reduction factor (F ≈ 0.36 for 30+ outlets).
formulas.decision: If hf × F > 20% of inlet pressure, upsize diameter or split lateral.
formulas.notes: Source: ASABE EP458; Hazen-Williams. Apply F factor for multi-outlet.
formulas.purpose_ar: تحديد قطر الخطوط الجانبية للحفاظ على تجانس الضغط.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Drip System Application Rate
formulas.variables:
I = application rate (mm/h)
q = emitter flow (L/h)
e = emitter efficiency (1.0 for 100%)
S₁ = emitter spacing on lateral (m)
S₂ = lateral spacing (m)
formulas.purpose: Calculate how fast drip applies water for scheduling.
formulas.example: q=4 L/h, S₁=0.3m, S₂=1.5m → I = 60×4×1/(0.3×1.5) = 533 mm/h (very high — drip is concentrated).
formulas.pitfall: Comparing drip I to sprinkler I — drip applies to small wetted volume only.
formulas.decision: Convert I to total volume: V = I × t × area / 60.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب سرعة تطبيق الماء بالتنقيط.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

chapters.title: IPCC 2019 Emission Models
chapters.intro: Tier 1/2 emission factors from IPCC 2019 Refinement — N₂O, CH₄, soil carbon.

formulas.name: N₂O Direct Emission (IPCC Tier 1)
formulas.variables:
F = N input (kg N/ha)
EF = emission factor (0.01 default)
44/28 = N₂O-N → N₂O molecular weight conversion
N₂O = emissions (kg N₂O/ha)
formulas.purpose: Estimate direct nitrous oxide emissions from fertilizer — 298× GWP.
formulas.example: 200 kg N/ha → N₂O = 200×0.01×1.57 = 3.14 kg N₂O/ha = 936 kg CO₂e/ha.
formulas.pitfall: Forgetting the 44/28 conversion; using old EF=0.0125 instead of 2019 value 0.01.
formulas.decision: Use enhanced-efficiency fertilizer to cut EF by 35% (IPCC 2019 Table 11.1).
formulas.notes: Source: IPCC 2019 Refinement, Vol. 4 Ch. 11, Eq. 11.1.
formulas.purpose_ar: تقدير انبعاثات N₂O من الأسمدة.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: CH₄ from Rice Paddies (IPCC)
formulas.variables:
SF = seasonal flux (kg CH₄/ha/day)
CF = conversion factor for water regime
t = cultivation period (days)
28 = GWP of CH₄ (AR5)
formulas.purpose: Methane emissions from flooded rice — major GHG in Asia.
formulas.example: SF=1.3, CF=0.78 (intermittent), t=110 → CH₄ = 1.3×0.78×110×28 = 3119 kg CO₂e/ha.
formulas.pitfall: Using continuously-flooded CF (1.0) for intermittent (0.78) or drained (0.27).
formulas.decision: Drain mid-season to cut CH₄ by 40-70%.
formulas.purpose_ar: انبعاثات الميثان من الأرز المغمور.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Enteric Fermentation CH₄ (Moe & Tyrrell)
formulas.variables:
DMI = dry matter intake (kg/day)
NDF% = neutral detergent fiber % of diet
CH₄ = methane (MJ/day)
formulas.purpose: Predict cattle methane from diet — key for dairy carbon footprint.
formulas.example: DMI=20 kg, NDF=45% → CH₄ = 3.41 + 1.02 - 4.19 = 0.24 MJ/day... (use IPCC Tier 2: Ym=6.5% × GE).
formulas.pitfall: Moe & Tyrrell is for dairy; IPCC Tier 2 (Ym=6.5%) is more general.
formulas.decision: Increase dietary starch (lower NDF) to reduce CH₄ by 10-20%.
formulas.notes: Source: Moe & Tyrrell (1979); IPCC 2019 Vol. 4 Ch. 10.
formulas.purpose_ar: التنبؤ بالميثان من النظام الغذائي للأبقار.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: N₂O Indirect (Volatilization + Leaching)
formulas.formula: N₂O_ind = (F × Frac_gas × EF_gas + F × Frac_leach × EF_leach) × 44/28
formulas.variables:
F = N input (kg N/ha)
Frac_gas = 0.10 (volatilized)
EF_gas = 0.010 (N₂O from NH₃/N0ₓ)
Frac_leach = 0.24 (leached)
EF_leach = 0.011 (N₂O from leaching)
formulas.purpose: Captures the N₂O that forms AFTER fertilizer leaves the field — ~40% of total.
formulas.pitfall: Forgetting indirect doubles total N₂O estimate; Frac_leach varies by rain.
formulas.decision: Split-apply N to cut Frac_leach; use inhibitors to cut Frac_gas.
formulas.purpose_ar: يلتقط N₂O الذي يتكون بعد مغادرة السماد للحقل.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Soil Carbon Stock Change (IPCC Tier 1)
formulas.variables:
SOC_ref = reference SOC stock (t C/ha, 0-30cm)
SOC_current = current stock
Area = hectares
20 = default inventory period (years)
formulas.purpose: Quantify soil carbon sequestration for carbon credit verification.
formulas.example: Convert to no-till: SOC_ref=50, SOC_current=40, Area=100ha → ΔSOC = 10×100/20 = 50 t C/yr = 183 t CO₂e/yr.
formulas.pitfall: Using wrong reference stock for climate/land-use zone.
formulas.decision: Use IPCC stock change factors (FLU, FMG, FI) for tier 1; measure for tier 3.
formulas.notes: Source: IPCC 2019, Vol. 4 Ch. 5, Eq. 5.1 (SOC stock method).
formulas.purpose_ar: تقدير تخزين الكربون للتحقق من ائتمانات الكربون.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

chapters.title: NRC Dairy Nutrition (2021)
chapters.intro: From Nutrient Requirements of Dairy Cattle — NEL, NDFD, methane, amino acid balance.

formulas.name: Net Energy for Lactation (NEL)
formulas.variables:
TDN = total digestible nutrients (% DM)
NEL = net energy lactation (Mcal/kg DM)
4.184 = Mcal to MJ conversion
formulas.purpose: Calculate energy available for milk production — NRC 2021 standard.
formulas.pitfall: Using DE or ME instead of NEL — overestimates by 15-25%.
formulas.decision: Balance ration at NEL ± 0.05 of cow requirement.
formulas.notes: Source: NRC (2021) Nutrient Requirements of Dairy Cattle, Eq. 3-10.
formulas.purpose_ar: حساب الطاقة المتاحة لإنتاج الحليب.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: NDF Digestibility (NRC 2021)
formulas.variables:
a = soluble fraction
b = insoluble but digestible fraction
c = rate of digestion (1/h)
t = incubation time (h)
formulas.purpose: Predict forage digestibility — 1% NDFD = +0.25 kg milk/day.
formulas.pitfall: Using 30h in vitro when ration model expects 48h; underestimates.
formulas.decision: Select hybrids with NDFD > 60% for high-producing cows.
formulas.purpose_ar: التنبؤ بهضم الأعلاف الخشنة.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Methane Prediction (IPCC Tier 2 — dairy)
formulas.variables:
GE = gross energy intake (MJ/day)
Ym = methane conversion factor (0.065 dairy, 0.070 beef)
0.0672 = MJ → kg CH₄ conversion
formulas.purpose: Daily cattle methane — required for dairy carbon footprint.
formulas.pitfall: Using Ym=0.07 for high-grain dairy (should be 0.045-0.060).
formulas.decision: Add lipids (3-5% of DM) to reduce Ym by 10-15%.
formulas.purpose_ar: ميثان الأبقار اليومي — مطللب للبصمة الكربونية.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Amino Acid Balance (Lys:Met)
formulas.variables:
Lys_supply = metabolizable lysine (g/day)
Met_supply = metabolizable methionine (g/day)
formulas.purpose: Balance the two most-limiting amino acids for milk protein.
formulas.example: Lys=180 g, Met=60 g → Ratio = 3.0. Target 3.0:1 (NRC 2021).
formulas.pitfall: Using total AA instead of metabolizable AA; ignoring microbial protein.
formulas.decision: Add rumen-protected Met if ratio > 3.2; protected Lys if ratio < 2.8.
formulas.purpose_ar: موازنة الأحماض الأمينية المحدودة لبروتين الحليب.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

chapters.title: Fertilizers Europe NUE Toolkit
chapters.intro: Nitrogen use efficiency, surplus, hidden N in residues.

formulas.name: Nitrogen Use Efficiency (NUE)
formulas.variables:
N_output = N in harvested product (kg N/ha)
N_input = fertilizer + biological + atmospheric N (kg N/ha)
formulas.purpose: Single metric for nitrogen performance — Fertilizers Europe target 50-90%.
formulas.example: Wheat: 150 kg N in grain / 180 kg N applied → NUE = 0.83 = 83%.
formulas.pitfall: Excluding soil N mineralization; counting only fertilizer N.
formulas.decision: NUE < 50%: surplus high, leaching risk. >90%: mining soil N.
formulas.notes: Source: Fertilizers Europe (2015) NUE Toolkit; EU Nitrogen Expert Panel.
formulas.purpose_ar: مقياس واحد لأداء النيتروجين.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: N Surplus (Fertilizers Europe)
formulas.variables:
N_input = total N inputs (kg N/ha)
N_output = N in harvested product + residue N (kg N/ha)
formulas.purpose: Environmental pressure metric — surpluses >80 kg N/ha risk leaching.
formulas.example: Wheat: input=180, output=150 → surplus=30 kg N/ha (safe). Maize: input=250, output=160 → surplus=90 (leaching risk).
formulas.pitfall: Forgetting biological N fixation (legumes add 50-300 kg N/ha).
formulas.decision: Surplus <50: sustainable. 50-80: caution. >80: action required.
formulas.notes: Source: EU Nitrogen Expert Panel (2015); Fertilizers Europe.
formulas.purpose_ar: مقياس الضغط البيئي — الفائض يخاطر بالترشيح.
formulas.part: Trusted-Reference Formulas (FAO-56, USDA-NRCS, ASABE, IPCC, NRC)

formulas.name: Hidden N in Crop Residues
formulas.variables:
Yield = grain yield (kg/ha)
HI = harvest index (0.4-0.5 cereals)
N_conc_residue = N concentration in residue (~0.5-1.0%)
formulas.purpose: Often-missing N input — residues mineralize 5-20% of their N next season.
formulas.pitfall: Assuming 100% of residue N mineralizes year 1 (actual 5-20%).
formulas.decision: Credit residue N at 15% mineralization: 19.8×0.15 = 3 kg N/ha credit.
